content: Let me start by reading the instructions and context files, then systematically audit all the Lean files in the project.
I'll start by reading the lean-auditor instructions and project context, then systematically audit all the Lean files.

1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning
11	    fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
12	
13	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
14	      - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter
15	        (the prover phase committed no edits — check via
16	        `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);
17	      - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.
18	
19	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
20	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
21	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
22	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
23	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
24	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
25	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
26	    to read. That's it.
27	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
28	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
29	    structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor
30	    subagent) next iter.
31	  - I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate
32	    Lean ↔ blueprint checker that compares Lean against the
33	    chapter — run both for full coverage when both are present.
34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
190

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 2): the chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 2** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships the
13	load-bearing chart-ring iso
14	
15	```
16	HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i)
17	  ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar
18	```
19	
20	together with the `kbar`-algebra preservation lemma. The forward direction is via
21	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`; the inverse via
22	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i = isLocalizationElem`.
23	
24	Upstream stratum: `BareScheme`. Downstream strata: `Points`, `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The chart-ring iso: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` -/
39	
40	/-- **The "other" `Fin 2` index** used in the chart-`i` affine coordinate `X (other i) / X i`. -/
41	def otherFin : Fin 2 → Fin 2
42	  | 0 => 1
43	  | 1 => 0
44	
45	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_zero : otherFin 0 = 1 := rfl
46	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_one : otherFin 1 = 0 := rfl
47	
48	private lemma otherFin_ne (i : Fin 2) : otherFin i ≠ i := by
49	  fin_cases i <;> decide
50	
51	/-- **The chart-`i` evaluation `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄`**: sends
52	`X i ↦ 1` and `X (otherFin i) ↦ X ()`. -/
53	private noncomputable def chartEvalRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
54	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
55	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
56	    (fun j : Fin 2 => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) else MvPolynomial.X ())
57	
58	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_self (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
59	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X i) = 1 := by
60	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
61	
62	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_other (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
63	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i)) = MvPolynomial.X () := by
64	  unfold chartEvalRingHom
65	  rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
66	  exact if_neg (otherFin_ne i)
67	
68	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_C (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) (r : kbar) :
69	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) = MvPolynomial.C r := by
70	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
71	
72	/-- **The forward direction of the chart-ring iso**: `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* k̄[u]` via
73	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`. -/
74	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
75	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
76	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) →+*
77	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
78	  (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
79	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
80	      (by rw [chartEvalRingHom_X_self]; exact isUnit_one)).comp
81	    (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
82	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
83	      (Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))
84	
85	/-- **The base ring map `k̄ →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)`** — the composite
86	`k̄ → 𝒜 0 → Away 𝒜 (X i)` of the algebra map into degree-`0` with `fromZeroRingHom`. -/
87	private noncomputable def kbarToAwayRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
88	    kbar →+*
89	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
90	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
91	  (HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
92	    (Submonoid.powers (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))).comp
93	    (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
94	
95	/-- **The inverse direction of the chart-ring iso**: `k̄[u] →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)` via the
96	universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`, sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i`. -/
97	noncomputable def mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
98	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
99	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+*
100	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
101	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
102	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i)
103	    (fun _ : Unit =>
104	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
105	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
106	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)))
107	
108	/-- Round-trip on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`: `forward ∘ inverse = id`. -/
109	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
110	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i).comp
111	        (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) =
112	      RingHom.id (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
113	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
114	  · intro r
115	    simp only [RingHom.id_apply, mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway,
116	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C, kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply,
117	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
118	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply]
119	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
120	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
121	        (Localization.mk ((algebraMap kbar
122	            ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0) r : _) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
123	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^0, ⟨0, rfl⟩⟩) =
124	      MvPolynomial.C r
125	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
126	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
127	    simp [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap, chartEvalRingHom]
128	  · intro _
129	    simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply,
130	      mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X',
131	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
132	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
133	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
134	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
135	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
136	        (Localization.mk ((MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) :
137	            MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ))
138	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ), ⟨1, rfl⟩⟩) =
139	      MvPolynomial.X ()
140	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
141	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
142	    simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_other, pow_one]
143	
144	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
145	
146	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
147	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
148	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
149	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
150	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.
151	
152	Proof structure (iter-172):
153	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
154	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
155	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
156	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`
157	     where `k = a₀` if i=1 else `a₁`. Hence it's `f (X ()^k)`.
158	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away
159	     (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀) = algebraMap kbar Away r₀ = f (C r₀)` since `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)`
160	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
161	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
162	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
163	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
164	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by
165	  classical
166	  -- We avoid `set 𝒜 := ...` here because it causes type-class friction with
167	  -- `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` and the `SetLike.GradeZero` coercion machinery.
168	  have hfi : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
169	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i
170	  have hgi : (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
171	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
172	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
173	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
174	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
175	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
176	  have hxd : ∀ j, v j ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (dv j) := by
177	    intro j; fin_cases j <;> exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X _ _
178	  -- Step 1: `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) (range v) = ⊤` (i.e. {X 0, X 1} generates `k̄[X 0, X 1]`
179	  -- over `𝒜 0`). We isolate the induction inside a `have` to avoid motive contamination.
180	  have hx : Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0) (Set.range v) = ⊤ := by
181	    apply top_unique
182	    intro p _
183	    refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX
184	    · -- C case: MvPolynomial.C r ∈ adjoin via algebraMap_mem.
185	      intro r
186	      have h : (algebraMap ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
187	          (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
188	          ⟨MvPolynomial.C r, MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C _ _⟩ = MvPolynomial.C r :=
189	        SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply _ _
190	      rw [← h]
191	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem _ _
192	    · -- add case
193	      intro p₁ p₂ hp₁ hp₂
194	      exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp₁ hp₂
195	    · -- mul_X case
196	      intro p₁ j hp₁
197	      refine Subalgebra.mul_mem _ hp₁ (Algebra.subset_adjoin ⟨j, ?_⟩)
198	      fin_cases j <;> simp [v]
199	  -- Step 2: Apply the Mathlib theorem.
200	  have htop := HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top hfi (ι' := Fin 2)
201	    v hx dv hxd
202	  -- Key intermediate: surjectivity of `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)` (constants → degree-0 piece).
203	  -- Used for the `algebraMap` case of the adjoin-induction below.
204	  have hkbar_sur : Function.Surjective
205	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar) 0)) := by
206	    rintro ⟨v, hv⟩
207	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
208	    apply Subtype.ext
209	    rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
210	    have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
211	    simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
212	    exact key
213	  -- Helper for the `mem` case: each generator equals `isLocalizationElem^k` for some `k`.
214	  -- The numerator `X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁` of degree `a = a₀ + a₁`, denominator `X i^a`.
215	  -- After simplification: this equals `(X (otherFin i) / X i)^(a_{otherFin i})`
216	  -- where `k = a₁` if `i = 0` and `k = a₀` if `i = 1`.
217	  have gen_eq_pow : ∀ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
218	      (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1)
219	      (hP : (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (a • 1)),
220	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) hP =
221	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi)^(ai (otherFin i)) := by
222	    intro a ai hai _hai_le hP
223	    apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
224	    have ha_eq : ai 0 + ai 1 = a := by
225	      have h := hai
226	      simp only [Fin.sum_univ_two, smul_eq_mul, dv, Matrix.cons_val_zero,
227	        Matrix.cons_val_one] at h
228	      omega
229	    -- Step A: compute LHS `.val` via `Away.val_mk`.
230	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
231	    -- Step B: compute RHS via `val_pow` + `Away.val_mk`. Use explicit `change` to make the
232	    -- isLocalizationElem definitionally visible.
233	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow]
234	    change _ = (HomogeneousLocalization.val (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
235	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi 1
236	        (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) ^ 1) _))^(ai (otherFin i))
237	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_pow]
238	    simp only [pow_one]
239	    -- Now both sides are `Localization.mk`. Reduce via `mk_eq_mk_iff` + `r_iff_exists`.
240	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]
241	    refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
242	    -- Goal: 1 * (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) * ↑(⟨X i, _⟩^(ai (otherFin i))) =
243	    --       1 * X (otherFin i)^(ai (otherFin i)) * X i^a
244	    -- Use that `↑(⟨X i, _⟩^k) = X i^k` (defeq via `SubmonoidClass.coe_pow` + Subtype.val).
245	    -- Case-split via `Fin.ext + omega` to get clean `0`/`1` for `i`.
246	    have hi_val : i.val = 0 ∨ i.val = 1 := by omega
247	    rcases hi_val with hi | hi
248	    · -- i = 0, otherFin 0 = 1
249	      have heq_i : i = (0 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
250	      subst heq_i
251	      simp only [otherFin_zero, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
252	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
253	      -- Goal: X 0^(ai 1) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 0^a * X 1^(ai 1)
254	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
255	    · -- i = 1, otherFin 1 = 0
256	      have heq_i : i = (1 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
257	      subst heq_i
258	      simp only [otherFin_one, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
259	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
260	      -- Goal: X 1^(ai 0) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 1^a * X 0^(ai 0)
261	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
262	  -- Step 4: surjectivity. Every y is in `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {generators}` = ⊤.
263	  intro y
264	  have hy_in : y ∈ Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
265	      { x | ∃ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
266	          (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1),
267	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a
268	          (∏ j, v j ^ ai j)
269	          (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i) = x } := by
270	    rw [htop]; trivial
271	  refine Algebra.adjoin_induction
272	      (p := fun y _ => y ∈ Set.range (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i))
273	      ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ hy_in
274	  · -- mem case
275	    rintro x ⟨a, ai, hai, hai_le, rfl⟩
276	    have hgen :=
277	      gen_eq_pow a ai hai hai_le (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i)
278	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.X ()^(ai (otherFin i)), ?_⟩
279	    rw [hgen]
280	    have hX : (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.X ()) =
281	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi := by
282	      change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()) = _
283	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
284	    rw [map_pow, hX]
285	  · -- algebraMap case
286	    intro r
287	    -- r : ↥(𝒜 0). Find `r₀ ∈ kbar` with `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀ = r`.
288	    obtain ⟨r₀, hr₀⟩ := hkbar_sur r
289	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C r₀, ?_⟩
290	    -- Goal: mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r₀) = algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r
291	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.C r₀) = _
292	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
293	    change kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r₀ = _
294	    simp only [kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply]
295	    rw [hr₀]
296	    rfl
297	  · -- add case
298	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
299	    exact ⟨pu + pw, by rw [map_add, hpu, hpw]⟩
300	  · -- mul case
301	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
302	    exact ⟨pu * pw, by rw [map_mul, hpu, hpw]⟩
303	
304	/-- Round-trip on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`: `inverse ∘ forward = id`.
305	
306	Closed by the "cancel surjective" route per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q2: from
307	`mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` (surjectivity of `inverse`) +
308	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right` (`forward ∘ inverse = id` on `MvPoly Unit kbar`),
309	conclude `inverse ∘ forward = id` on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`. The cancellation step itself is
310	mechanical; the only remaining substance is the surjectivity helper above. -/
311	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
312	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
313	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
314	      RingHom.id _ := by
315	  ext x
316	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
317	  -- Goal: ((mvPoly←Away) ∘ (Away→mvPoly)) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = (mvPoly←Away) p
318	  -- The inner `(Away→mvPoly) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = p` by aux_right; the result follows.
319	  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
320	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
321	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
322	  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]
323	
324	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
325	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
326	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
327	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
328	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
329	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
330	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
331	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
332	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
333	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
334	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
335	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
336	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
337	
338	/-- **`kbar`-algebra preservation of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`** (iter-174 Sub-task A
339	helper per `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3 step 3). The forward map
340	`Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar` carries `algebraMap kbar Away` to
341	`algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` (i.e. `MvPolynomial.C`).
342	
343	Closed via the inverse's `eval₂Hom_C` action: `inverse (C r) = kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r =
344	algebraMap kbar Away r` (the last identity is definitional, by the `algebraKbarAway`
345	`Algebra.compHom` instance combined with `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`). The
346	forward round-trip then gives `forward (algebraMap kbar Away r) = C r`. -/
347	lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap
348	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
349	    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
350	        (algebraMap kbar
351	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
352	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
353	      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
354	  ext r
355	  -- `algebraMap kbar Away r = mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r)` since
356	  -- the `algebraKbarAway` instance is `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` and
357	  -- `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away = HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _` (see
358	  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`), so the composite is exactly `kbarToAwayRingHom`
359	  -- which equals `inverse (C r)` by `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`.
360	  have hinv : mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) =
361	      algebraMap kbar
362	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
363	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) r := by
364	    unfold mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
365	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
366	    rfl
367	  -- Now apply forward (= homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly) and use aux_right's roundtrip.
368	  have hround : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
369	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)) =
370	      MvPolynomial.C r :=
371	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)
372	  -- Combine: rewrite the inverse C r as algebraMap kbar Away r, then apply roundtrip.
373	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingEquiv.toRingHom_eq_coe,
374	    RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso, RingEquiv.ofRingHom_apply]
375	  rw [← hinv, hround]
376	  rfl
377	
378	/-! ### `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instance via the 2-chart cover -/
379	
380	/-- **Helper for `projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux`**: parameterized by `f := X i`,
381	the chart morphism `Spec(Away 𝒜 f) → Spec k̄` is smooth of relative dimension `1`.
382	
383	Proof: rewrite the composition `awayι ≫ Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar (𝒜 0))`
384	via `awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` to a single `Spec.map (kbarToAwayRingHom)`,
385	then apply `HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` + `RingHom.locally_of` to reduce to
386	`RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 kbarToAwayRingHom`, which equals
387	`algebraMap kbar (Away)`. Close via `of_algEquiv` with `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) kbar` (using
388	`renameEquiv finOneEquiv` + the symm of the chart-ring iso upgraded to a `kbar`-algEquiv). -/
389	private lemma projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_X (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
390	    (i : Fin 2) :
391	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
392	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
393	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos ≫
394	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom) := by
395	  -- Step 1: rewrite the composition as `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom kbarToAwayRingHom)`.
396	  rw [show ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom : ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar ⟶ _)
397	      = Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
398	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
399	          (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))) from rfl]
400	  rw [show (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
401	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
402	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos ≫
403	        Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
404	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
405	            (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))) =
406	      (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
407	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
408	            (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos ≫
409	          Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)) ≫
410	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
411	          (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))) from
412	      (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm]
413	  rw [Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp]
414	  -- Step 2: now goal is on `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom kbarToAwayRingHom)`.
415	  rw [HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1)]
416	  apply RingHom.locally_of RingHom.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_respectsIso
417	  -- Step 3: goal is `RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (kbarToAwayRingHom)`,
418	  -- which equals `algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 (X i))` by definitional unfolding of `algebraKbarAway`.
419	  change RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1
420	    (algebraMap kbar (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
421	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))
422	  rw [RingHom.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_algebraMap]
423	  -- Step 4: build the `kbar`-algebra equivalence `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) kbar ≃ₐ[kbar] Away`
424	  -- and apply `of_algEquiv`. The chart-ring iso `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` ships
425	  -- `Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`; the `_algebraMap` lemma upgrades it to a `kbar`-algEquiv.
426	  -- Compose with `renameEquiv finOneEquiv` to transport across `Fin 1 ≃ Unit`.
427	  let eUnit : HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
428	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃ₐ[kbar] MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
429	    AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv (f := homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i) <| fun r =>
430	      RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap kbar i) r
431	  let eFin : MvPolynomial (Fin 1) kbar ≃ₐ[kbar] MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
432	    MvPolynomial.renameEquiv kbar finOneEquiv
433	  let e : MvPolynomial (Fin 1) kbar ≃ₐ[kbar]
434	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
435	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
436	    eFin.trans eUnit.symm
437	  exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algEquiv (n := 1) e
438	
439	private lemma projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
440	    (i : Fin 2) :
441	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i ≫
442	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom) := by
443	  -- The cover's `.f i` is `Proj.awayι 𝒜 (![X 0, X 1] i) ...`; after `fin_cases i`,
444	  -- `(![X 0, X 1] i)` reduces definitionally to `X i`, and the morphism matches the
445	  -- helper `projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_X`.
446	  simp only [Scheme.AffineOpenCover.openCover_f, projectiveLineBarAffineCover,
447	    Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan]
448	  fin_cases i
449	  · exact projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_X kbar 0
450	  · exact projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_X kbar 1
451

10	/-!
11	# Abelian-variety rigidity: genus-`0` final assembly
12	
13	This file is the **genus-`0` final layer** of the project's committed characteristic-free
14	route (route (c)) to abelian-variety rigidity. It assembles the headline
15	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — the char-free replacement (no `[CharZero kbar]`) for
16	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` (which remains in
17	tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
18	
19	The abstract Mumford Rigidity Lemma chain (`rigidity_lemma`) and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries
20	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` = Cor 1.5, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` = Cor 1.2) it
21	implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That
22	chain is PROVEN axiom-clean (iters 157–162); this file consumes it.
23	
24	The final assembly has three links specific to the genus-`0` route:
25	
26	1. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
27	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
28	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
29	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
30	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
31	2. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
32	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
33	3. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
34	
35	The file also exposes the project-local bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` — the dominance of the
36	canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion — which becomes provable once Lane A
37	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) ships the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1`.
38	
39	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
40	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
41	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
42	
43	## Encoding notes
44	
45	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so the projective line is encoded by its
46	abstract characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of
47	relative dimension `1` with `genus = 0` (see
48	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ProjectiveLineBar`).
49	The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional** (`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover
50	may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled. Declaration 3 is pinned verbatim to
51	`rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`, because it is the exact signature the
52	consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/

65	/-! ### Iter-167 dominance bridge for the canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` map
66	
67	The four product / Proj instances `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux` needs
68	(`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`, `IsReduced` of the product, and
69	`IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left`) all ship from Lane A
70	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) as the instances `projGm_geomIrred`,
71	`projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`, `projGm_isReduced`, and `projectiveLineBar_isReduced`. The
72	helper resolves them all by `infer_instance`.
73	
74	The one remaining bridge — dominance of the canonical inclusion `ι : Gm ⟶ ℙ¹` — is
75	file-local because its proof depends on the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1` (still
76	a Lane A scaffold sorry through iter-167). It is exposed here as a named top-level lemma
77	`iotaGm_isDominant` so the helper carries no inline `sorry`. -/
78	
79	/-- **Sub-task (b) range containment (iter-190 Lane E refactor):** the topological image of
80	the `k̄`-point `onePt = [1:1] ∈ ℙ¹` lies inside the open image of the chart-1 affine open
81	`Proj.awayι (X 1) : Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1)) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`.
82	
83	**Mathematical content.** `onePt.left = Proj.fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 (evalIntoGlobal v) _`
84	with `v 0 = v 1 = 1`. Since `evalIntoGlobal v (X 1) = (ΓSpecIso k̄).inv 1 = 1` is a unit in
85	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`, the preimage of `D₊(X 1) ⊆ Proj 𝒜` under `onePt.left` is
86	`(Spec k̄).basicOpen 1 = ⊤`, so the image of `onePt.left` lies entirely in
87	`Set.range (Proj.awayι (X 1)) = D₊(X 1)`.
88	
89	The range chain (iter-184 closure):
90	(i) `Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange` + `Proj.opensRange_awayι` rewrite the RHS into
91	`↑(Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 (X 1))`; (ii) pointwise destructure to `onePt.left x ∈ basicOpen`;
92	(iii) `Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage` + `change` expose `onePt.left = fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 φ hφ`
93	(`pointOfVec` unfolds through `Over.homMk` even though `evalIntoGlobal`/`pointOfVec` are
94	private — defeq is unaffected by name visibility); (iv) `fromOfGlobalSections_preimage_basicOpen`
95	reduces to `(Spec k̄).basicOpen ((ΓSpecIso k̄).inv.hom (eval (·=1) (X 1)))`;
96	(v) `Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit` + `simp` closes via `eval_X` + `map_one` + `isUnit_one`.
97	Axiom-clean: `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.
98	
99	Iter-190: extracted from the previous `∃`-packed `iotaGm_onePt_chart1_factor` (verdict A of
100	the iter-189 mathlib-analogist Lane E `projappiso` analysis) so the chart-1 lift
101	`iotaGm_r_1` is exposed as a named `noncomputable def`. Downstream
102	`iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` can then apply `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app`
103	directly to `iotaGm_r_1` without re-deriving the range witness via `cancel_mono`. -/
104	private lemma iotaGm_r_1_range_subset (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
105	    Set.range ⇑(ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left ⊆
106	      Set.range ⇑(Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
107	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
108	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)
109	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
110	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)) := by
111	  -- Iter-190 prover closure (point-witness reshape). The iter-184 closed body relied on
112	  -- `rw [← Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]` to expose the RHS `Set.range` as a `(...).opensRange`
113	  -- coercion; in the standalone signature here, elaboration types the LHS at
114	  -- `Set ↥(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` whereas `Proj.awayι _ _ _ _` is typed at
115	  -- `Set ↥(Proj 𝒜)`, so the rewrite pattern does not match. We sidestep this by
116	  -- destructuring point-wise FIRST, then `change`-ing the membership goal into
117	  -- `(...).opensRange.1` (a defeq reshape that works because the underlying carriers
118	  -- `↥(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` and `↥(Proj 𝒜)` are defeq through
119	  -- `Scheme.asOver` / `OverClass.asOver`). The remainder of the chain is identical to
120	  -- the iter-184 body.
121	  rintro _ ⟨x, rfl⟩
122	  change (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left.base x ∈ (Proj.awayι _ _ _ _).opensRange.1
123	  rw [Proj.opensRange_awayι]
124	  change x ∈ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left ⁻¹ᵁ Proj.basicOpen _ _
125	  change x ∈ Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) _ _ ⁻¹ᵁ _
126	  rw [Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_preimage_basicOpen _ _ _
127	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)
128	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)]
129	  refine (Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit _ ?_).symm ▸ TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_top x
130	  change IsUnit ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
131	    ((MvPolynomial.eval (fun _ : Fin 2 => (1 : kbar)))
132	      ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
133	        Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)))
134	  simp
135	
136	/-- **Sub-task (b) lift (iter-190 Lane E refactor):** the chart-1 factor
137	`r_1 : Spec k̄ ⟶ Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1))` of the `k̄`-point `onePt = [1:1] ∈ ℙ¹` through
138	the chart-1 open immersion `Proj.awayι (X 1)`. Provided by the universal property
139	`IsOpenImmersion.lift` of the open immersion `Proj.awayι (X 1)` together with the
140	range-containment witness `iotaGm_r_1_range_subset`.
141	
142	Iter-190 refactor: replaces the existential `∃ r_1, ...` of the previous
143	`iotaGm_onePt_chart1_factor` (verdict A of the iter-189 mathlib-analogist Lane E
144	`projappiso` analysis) with a named `noncomputable def`, so that the consumer
145	`iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` can apply `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app`
146	directly to `iotaGm_r_1` instead of re-deriving the lift identity via `cancel_mono`. -/
147	private noncomputable def iotaGm_r_1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
148	    Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶
149	      Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
150	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
151	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
152	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1))) :=
153	  IsOpenImmersion.lift
154	    (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
155	      ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
156	        Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)
157	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
158	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1))
159	    (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left
160	    (iotaGm_r_1_range_subset kbar)
161	
162	/-- **Sub-task (b) factorisation (iter-190 Lane E refactor):** the chart-1 factor
163	`iotaGm_r_1` factors `onePt.left` through `Proj.awayι (X 1)`. Discharges via
164	`IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac`. -/
165	private lemma iotaGm_r_1_fac (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
166	    iotaGm_r_1 kbar ≫ Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
167	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
168	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)
169	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
170	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1) =
171	      (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left :=
172	  IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _

174	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Proj.awayι` chart-evaluation infrastructure
175	
176	The blueprint `chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` (iter-195/iter-196 analogist recipe
177	`lane-e-proj-appiso-pivot`, ANALOGUE_FOUND) identifies two project-local Mathlib lemmas
178	that, together, evaluate `((Proj.awayι 𝒜 f).appIso ⊤).inv` on the canonical generator
179	`isLocElem ∈ Away 𝒜 f`. The two lemmas mirror the Mathlib pattern
180	`IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean:560-564`)
181	applied to the `Proj`-side basic-open factorisation.
182	
183	Each declaration below is project-local (general-purpose graded ring `𝒜 : ℕ → σ`); none
184	mention `kbar` or `projectiveLineBarGrading`. They are placed here as supplements
185	because they have no upstream Mathlib home (yet). -/
186	
187	/-- Project-local supplement: the preimage of `Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f` under
188	`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm` is `⊤`. Combines `Proj.opensRange_awayι` with
189	`Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange`. -/
190	lemma Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self
191	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
192	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
193	    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm ⁻¹ᵁ Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f = ⊤ := by
194	  rw [← Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm, Scheme.Hom.preimage_opensRange]
195	
196	/-- Project-local supplement: `Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm` factors as
197	`Spec.map (basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫ (isAffineOpen_basicOpen ...).fromSpec`.
198	
199	This is the "iter-195 analogist recipe step (i)" `Proj.awayι_isoSpec_compat` from
200	`analogies/lane-e-proj-appiso-pivot.md`. It is the bridge that lets us transport
201	Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` (which is about `Γ(X, U)`, not the
202	project's `Away 𝒜 f`) into a closed-form for `(Proj.awayι).app`. -/
203	lemma Proj.awayι_eq_specMap_fromSpec
204	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
205	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
206	    Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm =
207	      Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
208	      (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).fromSpec := by
209	  rw [Proj.awayι, IsAffineOpen.fromSpec, ← Category.assoc]
210	  congr 1
211	  -- Goal: `basicOpenIsoSpec.inv = Spec.map(basicOpenIsoAway.inv) ≫ isoSpec.inv`.
212	  -- Repackage RHS as the `inv` of a single `Iso.trans` so we can apply `Iso.ext`
213	  -- on the `.hom` side, where the `basicOpenIsoSpec_hom`/`basicOpenToSpec`/
214	  -- `isoSpec_hom = toSpecΓ` chain composes definitionally.
215	  rw [show (Spec.map (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
216	       (Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).isoSpec.inv) =
217	       ((Proj.isAffineOpen_basicOpen 𝒜 f f_deg hm).isoSpec ≪≫
218	         Scheme.Spec.mapIso (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).op).inv from rfl]
219	  congr 1
220	  apply Iso.ext
221	  rw [Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, Proj.basicOpenToSpec, Iso.trans_hom,
222	      IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom]
223	  rfl
224	
225	/-- **Iter-197 Lane E (blueprint `lem:basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top`).**
226	Closed-form for `(basicOpenIsoSpec).inv.app ⊤`: the inverse of the canonical
227	scheme iso `Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec`, evaluated on global sections, factors as
228	`topIso.hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ (ΓSpecIso _).inv`.
229	
230	Proven by `cancel_mono` on the iso `basicOpenIsoSpec.hom.appTop` (an iso because
231	its closed form via `basicOpenToSpec_app_top` is a composite of three isos:
232	`ΓSpecIso.hom`, `basicOpenIsoAway.hom`, `topIso.inv`). After cancellation both
233	sides reduce to `𝟙 Γ(basicOpen 𝒜 f, ⊤)` by `Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc` triples. -/
234	lemma Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top
235	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
236	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
237	    (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv.app ⊤ =
238	      (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.hom ≫
239	      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
240	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv := by
241	  set β := Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm with hβ
242	  set α := Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm with hα
243	  have hβh : Scheme.Hom.appTop β.hom =
244	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).hom ≫ α.hom ≫ (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).topIso.inv := by
245	    change Scheme.Hom.appTop (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom = _
246	    rw [Scheme.Hom.appTop, Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top,
247	        Proj.basicOpenIsoAway_hom]
248	  haveI : IsIso (Scheme.Hom.appTop β.hom) := by rw [hβh]; infer_instance
249	  change Scheme.Hom.appTop β.inv = _
250	  apply (cancel_mono (Scheme.Hom.appTop β.hom)).mp
251	  rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, β.hom_inv_id, Scheme.Hom.id_appTop, hβh]
252	  rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc, α.inv_hom_id_assoc,
253	      Iso.hom_inv_id]
254	
255	/-- **Iter-197 Lane E (blueprint `lem:awayι_app_basicOpen`).** Section-level
256	closed-form for `(Proj.awayι 𝒜 f).app (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f)`: the canonical
257	section pullback factors as `basicOpenIsoAway.inv ≫ (ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫
258	(Spec _).presheaf.map (eqToHom <preimage = ⊤>).op`. Port of Mathlib's
259	`IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` (`AffineScheme.lean:560-564`) to the
260	`Proj.awayι` direction.
261	
262	Proven via the definitional unfolding `awayι = basicOpenIsoSpec.inv ≫ ι`
263	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:189-190`), then
264	`Scheme.Hom.comp_app` + `Scheme.Opens.ι_app_self` + `Scheme.Hom.app_eq` on
265	the `ι_preimage_self` equation + the iter-197 named helper
266	`Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top`, then `aesop_cat` to collapse the
267	residual `eqToHom` cascade. -/
268	lemma Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen
269	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
270	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
271	    (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm).app (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f) =
272	      (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫
273	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫
274	      (Spec _).presheaf.map
275	        (eqToHom (Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen_self 𝒜 f f_deg hm)).op := by
276	  change ((Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv ≫ (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).ι).app
277	    (Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f) = _
278	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_app, Scheme.Opens.ι_app_self,
279	      Scheme.Hom.app_eq (Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec 𝒜 f f_deg hm).inv
280	        ((Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f).ι_preimage_self),
281	      Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top]
282	  aesop_cat
283	
284	/-- **Iter-197 Lane E (blueprint `lem:awayι_appIso_top_inv_apply_isLocElem`).**
285	Closed form for the inverse of `(Proj.awayι 𝒜 f).appIso ⊤`: the section-level
286	inverse iso factors as `(ΓSpecIso _).hom ≫ basicOpenIsoAway.hom ≫
287	presheaf.map(eqToHom <opensRange = basicOpen>).op`.
288	
289	Proven by computing `((Proj.awayι 𝒜 f).appIso ⊤).hom` via Mathlib's
290	`Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom` + our `Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen`, then inverting via
291	`Iso.inv_eq_inv`. After cancellation of the `eqToHom` cascade (which collapses
292	to `𝟙` on `Γ(Spec, ⊤)`), only the three iso-components survive: the inverse of
293	`basicOpenIsoAway` and `ΓSpecIso`, plus the eqToHom presheaf-map adjustment. -/
294	lemma Proj.awayι_appIso_top_inv
295	    {σ : Type*} {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
296	    (𝒜 : ℕ → σ) [GradedRing 𝒜] (f : A) {m : ℕ} (f_deg : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m) :
297	    ((Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm).appIso ⊤).inv =
298	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).hom ≫ (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f f_deg hm).hom ≫
299	        (Proj 𝒜).presheaf.map
300	          (eqToHom ((Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm)).trans
301	            (Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm))).op := by
302	  -- Compute via the .hom side: show `RHS ≫ appIso.hom = 𝟙`.
303	  refine Eq.symm ?_
304	  refine (Iso.comp_hom_eq_id _).mp ?_
305	  rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom,
306	      Scheme.Hom.app_eq (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm)
307	        ((Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm)).trans
308	          (Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm)),
309	      Proj.awayι_app_basicOpen]

63	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
64	
65	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
66	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
67	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
68	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
69	`K(X)`.
70	
71	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
72	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
73	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
74	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
75	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
76	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
77	-/
78	
79	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
80	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
81	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
82	
83	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
84	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
85	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
86	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
87	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
88	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
89	integrality field is needed.
90	
91	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
92	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
93	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
94	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
95	  point : X
96	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
97	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
98	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
99	
100	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
101	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,
102	`Div(X) = ⨁_{Y prime divisor of X} ℤ · Y`. An element `D = Σ nᵢ · Yᵢ` with finitely
103	many nonzero coefficients is a **Weil divisor**; if all `nᵢ ≥ 0` it is **effective**.
104	
105	Blueprint reference: `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130). -/
106	def Scheme.WeilDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ
107	
108	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
109	
110	noncomputable instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : AddCommGroup X.WeilDivisor :=
111	  inferInstanceAs (AddCommGroup (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
112	
113	instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : Inhabited X.WeilDivisor :=
114	  inferInstanceAs (Inhabited (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
115	
116	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
117	
118	/-! ## §2. Order of a rational function at a prime divisor
119	
120	For a scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, every prime divisor `Y` carries a discrete
121	valuation `v_Y` on the function field `K(X)`. The order of a nonzero rational
122	function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along `Y` is the integer `ord_Y(f) := v_Y(f)`. -/
123	
124	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
125	
126	/-- **Order of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along a prime divisor `Y`.**
127	
128	For `X` satisfying Hartshorne's `(*)`, the local ring `O_{X,η}` at the generic
129	point `η` of `Y` is a discrete valuation ring with quotient field the function
130	field `K(X)`, and `ord_Y(f) = v_Y(f) ∈ ℤ` is the value of the associated normalised
131	discrete valuation on `f`.
132	
133	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
134	divisor and `ord_P(f) = v_P(f)` is the standard DVR valuation at `P`.
135	
136	Blueprint reference: `def:order_at_point` (Hartshorne II §6 pp. 130–131; Stacks 02ME).
137	
138	iter-176 body (per analogist `dvr-rationalmap-order`): the body uses Mathlib's
139	`Ring.ordFrac` (the canonical `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero hom from
140	`Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`, Stacks `02MD`) on the stalk
141	`X.presheaf.stalk Y.point`, then projects through `WithZero.log : ℤᵐ⁰ → ℤ`
142	(the canonical projection with junk-on-zero, `Mathlib.Algebra.GroupWithZero.WithZero`).
143	On `f = 0` this gives `order Y 0 = 0` (junk convention from `WithZero.log_zero`).
144	
145	The required Mathlib typeclasses on the stalk are:
146	- `IsNoetherianRing` — from `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`.
147	- `IsDomain` ⟹ `Nontrivial` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
148	- `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) X.functionField` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
149	- `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` — threaded explicitly (the
150	  topological-coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge `Order.coheight Y.point = 1 ⟹
151	  Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` is a Mathlib-upstream-pending
152	  gap; see Stacks `02IZ` / `005X`). -/
153	noncomputable def order {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
154	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
155	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
156	    (f : X.functionField) : ℤ :=
157	  WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)
158	
159	end Scheme.RationalMap

161	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
162	
163	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
164	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
165	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
166	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
167	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
168	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
169	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
170	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/
171	
172	/-- Project-bespoke class encoding Hartshorne's "regular in codimension one"
173	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk
174	`O_{X,Y.point}`. The `[IsIntegral X]` precondition makes
175	`IsDomain (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` available so that the
176	`IsDiscreteValuationRing` predicate is well-formed
177	(`AlgebraicGeometry.instIsDomainCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsIntegral`). -/
178	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) [IsIntegral X] : Prop where
179	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk is a discrete valuation
180	  ring. (This is the precise content of Hartshorne's `(*)`; the weaker
181	  `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` derives via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹
182	  `IsPrincipalIdealRing` ⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain via the bridge
183	  instance below.) -/
184	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
185	
186	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
187	synthesis can derive `IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for
188	every prime divisor `Y`. -/
189	instance Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instIsDiscreteValuationRingStalk
190	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
191	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
192	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
193	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
194	
195	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
196	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
197	prime divisor `Y`, via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹ `IsPrincipalIdealRing`
198	⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain (Mathlib's `IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing`
199	+ `IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one`). -/
200	instance Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instKrullDimLEStalk
201	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
202	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
203	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
204	  haveI : IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
205	    Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
206	  haveI : IsPrincipalIdealRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
207	    IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing
208	  IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one _
209	
210	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.1**: for a nonzero rational function `f` on a Noetherian
211	integral scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, the order function `Y ↦ ord_Y(f)` is
212	nonzero at only finitely many prime divisors `Y`. This is the well-definedness
213	side condition for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`.
214	
215	iter-177 status: this packages Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, which Mathlib does not
216	ship. The body is a Mathlib-upstream-pending gap (Stacks tag `02RV` — for a
217	nonzero element `f ∈ K(X)^×` of a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely
218	many height-one primes can divide either numerator or denominator); the proof
219	factors through `IsLocallyNoetherian X` + the principal-ideal generation of
220	height-1 primes + the finite irreducible-component decomposition of
221	`V(f₀) ∪ V(f∞)`. The chapter pins this as a separate sub-build deferral
222	(`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5).
223	
224	The statement is generic in `f` (no `f ≠ 0` hypothesis is threaded): on
225	`f = 0` the function `Y ↦ ord_Y(0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` has empty support,
226	which is finite, so the conclusion holds vacuously. -/
227	private theorem rationalMap_order_finite_support {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
228	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
229	    (f : X.functionField) :
230	    (Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor =>
231	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite := by
232	  -- **iter-192 case split + f = 0 branch closed axiom-clean.**
233	  --
234	  -- Case 1 (f = 0): the order function evaluates to
235	  -- `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` at every
236	  -- prime divisor, so the support is empty (finite vacuously).
237	  by_cases hf : f = 0
238	  · subst hf
239	    convert Set.finite_empty
240	    ext Y
241	    simp only [Function.mem_support, ne_eq, Set.mem_empty_iff_false, iff_false,
242	      Decidable.not_not, Scheme.RationalMap.order, map_zero, WithZero.log_zero]
243	  -- Case 2 (f ≠ 0): genuinely needs Hartshorne II.6.1 (Stacks 02RV).
244	  -- The proof for nonzero `f` requires the substrate
245	  -- "for a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely many height-1
246	  -- primes can divide the numerator or denominator of a nonzero rational
247	  -- function" — a Mathlib-pending gap noted in the chapter docstring.
248	  -- Body owed iter-193+; iter-192 closes only the f = 0 vacuous case.
249	  · sorry
