251	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
252	
253	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
254	
255	/-! ## §3. Divisor of a closed point on a curve
256	
257	On a smooth proper curve `C` over a field, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
258	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral
259	scheme `C`). The associated Weil divisor is `[P] = 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`. -/
260	
261	/-- **The Weil divisor associated to a closed point `P` on a curve.** The element
262	`[P] := 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`, i.e. the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one.
263	
264	For a smooth proper curve every closed point is automatically a prime divisor (it
265	is a codimension-one integral closed subscheme of the one-dimensional integral
266	scheme `C`); conversely every prime divisor on a curve is of this form, so an
267	arbitrary divisor on `C` is a finite formal `ℤ`-linear combination
268	`Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` of closed points.
269	
270	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_closed_point` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137).
271	
272	iter-174 body: the function is junk-defined outside its intended scope. We
273	case-split on `Order.coheight P = 1` (the codimension-one witness of
274	`PrimeDivisor`). On the branch where the equality holds — automatic for a
275	closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme — we promote `P` to a
276	`PrimeDivisor` via the witness and return `Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1`, i.e.
277	the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one. On the off-branch (junk regime)
278	we return the zero divisor. The blueprint pins the well-definedness argument
279	"`IsClosed {P}` on a one-dimensional integral scheme ⟹ `coheight P = 1`" as a
280	separate threadable hypothesis at the call site (chapter L330–L340 "Lean
281	signature scope"); see `ofClosedPoint_eq_single` for the equation in the
282	hypothesised regime. -/
283	noncomputable def ofClosedPoint {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
284	    (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) : C.WeilDivisor :=
285	  if h : Order.coheight P = 1 then Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 else 0
286	
287	/-- In the hypothesised regime where the closed point `P` has coheight one
288	(the codim-1 condition automatic for a closed point of a one-dimensional
289	integral scheme), `ofClosedPoint P hP` is the prime divisor `P` with
290	multiplicity one. -/
291	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_single {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
292	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P = 1) :
293	    ofClosedPoint P hP = Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 := by
294	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
295	
296	/-- Off-branch: outside the codim-1 regime, `ofClosedPoint` is junk-defined as
297	the zero divisor. (Only relevant when the user supplies a "closed point" that
298	does not have coheight one in the ambient scheme — e.g. a generic point or a
299	codim-≥2 point on a higher-dimensional scheme.) -/
300	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
301	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P ≠ 1) :
302	    ofClosedPoint P hP = 0 := by
303	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
304	
305	/-! ## §4. Degree of a divisor over an algebraically closed base -/
306	
307	/-- **Degree of a Weil divisor on a smooth proper curve over `k̄`.**
308	
309	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every closed point of a smooth proper
310	curve `C` has residue field `k̄`, so each prime divisor `[P]` contributes degree
311	one to the sum, and `deg(D) := Σᵢ nᵢ` is the sum of the integer coefficients of
312	the formal sum `D = Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]`.
313	
314	(Over a general field `k` one weights by the residue-field degrees
315	`Σᵢ nᵢ · [κ(Pᵢ) : k]` to recover the geometric degree; the project's RR bridge
316	needs only the `k̄` specialisation.)
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137; Stacks 0BE0).
319	
320	Implementation: `Finsupp.sum D (fun _ n => n)` is the sum of all coefficients of
321	the finitely supported function representing `D`. -/
322	noncomputable def degree (D : X.WeilDivisor) : ℤ :=
323	  (D : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n)
324	
325	/-- **The degree map is a group homomorphism `Div(C) → ℤ`.**
326	
327	`deg(D₁ + D₂) = deg(D₁) + deg(D₂)`, `deg(-D) = -deg(D)`, `deg(0) = 0`. Bundled
328	as an `AddMonoidHom` for downstream use (the linear-equivalence quotient
329	`Cl(C) := Div(C) / im(div)` will inherit a `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ` from this).
330	
331	Blueprint reference: `thm:divisor_degree_hom` (immediate from `def:divisor_degree`).
332	
333	Implementation: built from `Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)`, the
334	generic Mathlib packaging that lifts a family of `AddMonoidHom`s indexed by the
335	support into a single `AddMonoidHom` on the finsupp. Unfolds to
336	`D.sum (fun _ z ↦ z) = degree D` (see `degree_hom_apply` below). -/
337	noncomputable def degree_hom : X.WeilDivisor →+ ℤ :=
338	  Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)
339	
340	@[simp]
341	lemma degree_hom_apply (D : X.WeilDivisor) : degree_hom D = degree D :=
342	  Finsupp.liftAddHom_apply (α := X.PrimeDivisor) (M := ℤ) (N := ℤ)
343	    (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ) D
344	
345	/-- **Degree of the zero divisor is zero.** A direct consequence of
346	`degree_hom` being an `AddMonoidHom`. -/
347	@[simp]
348	lemma degree_zero : degree (0 : X.WeilDivisor) = 0 := by
349	  rw [← degree_hom_apply]; exact map_zero _
350	
351	/-- **Degree is additive.** A direct consequence of `degree_hom` being an
352	`AddMonoidHom`. -/
353	lemma degree_add (D₁ D₂ : X.WeilDivisor) :
354	    degree (D₁ + D₂) = degree D₁ + degree D₂ := by
355	  rw [← degree_hom_apply, ← degree_hom_apply, ← degree_hom_apply]
356	  exact map_add _ _ _
357	
358	/-! ## §5. Principal divisors -/
359	
360	/-- **The principal divisor of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×`.**
361	
362	By Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, `ord_Y(f) = 0` for all but finitely many prime
363	divisors `Y`, so the formal sum
364	`div(f) := Σ_{Y prime divisor} ord_Y(f) · Y ∈ Div(X)`
365	has finite support and is a well-defined Weil divisor.
366	
367	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, this specialises to
368	`div(f) = Σ_{P closed point} ord_P(f) · [P]`.
369	
370	Blueprint reference: `def:principal_divisor` (Hartshorne II §6 Lemma 6.1 +
371	following definition, p. 131).
372	
373	iter-177 body: the construction uses `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` with the
374	finite-support witness `rationalMap_order_finite_support`. The latter is a
375	private theorem packaging Hartshorne 6.1; its body is a Mathlib-pending gap
376	(see chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5 sub-build note) and is left as
377	a `sorry` for an iter-178+ Mathlib-upstream PR. -/
378	noncomputable def principal [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
379	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField)
380	    (_hf : f ≠ 0) : X.WeilDivisor :=
381	  Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
382	    (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
383	    (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)
384	
385	/-- **The coefficient of `principal f hf` at a prime divisor `Y` is the order of
386	`f` along `Y`.** This is the basic structural unfolding of the
387	`Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` packaging in `principal`; one-line via
388	`Finsupp.ofSupportFinite_coe` from `Mathlib.Data.Finsupp.Defs`.
389	
390	iter-193 substrate helper for the Lane I body close
391	(`degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`). -/
392	lemma principal_apply [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
393	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0)
394	    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) :
395	    (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y =
396	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f := by
397	  change (Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
398	      (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
399	      (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)) Y = _
400	  rw [Finsupp.ofSupportFinite_coe]
401	
402	/-- **`Scheme.RationalMap.order Y 1 = 0`** — the order of the constant
403	function `1` is `0` at every prime divisor. Direct from
404	`map_one` of `Ring.ordFrac` + `WithZero.log_one`.
405	
406	iter-193 substrate helper for further structural results
407	(`principal_one_eq_zero` and similar). -/
408	@[simp]
409	lemma _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order_one
410	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
411	    (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
412	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)] :
413	    Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (1 : X.functionField) = 0 := by
414	  unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
415	  rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
416	
417	/-- **The principal divisor of the constant function `1` is the zero
418	divisor.** A direct consequence of `Scheme.RationalMap.order_one`. -/
419	@[simp]
420	lemma principal_one [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
421	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
422	    principal (1 : X.functionField) one_ne_zero = 0 := by
423	  change (_ : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) = (0 : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
424	  apply Finsupp.ext
425	  intro Y
426	  rw [principal_apply]
427	  exact Scheme.RationalMap.order_one Y
428	
429	/-- **The principal-divisor map is a group homomorphism `K(X)^× → Div(X)`.**
430	
431	Concretely `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, `div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, `div(1) = 0`. Bundled
432	as a `MonoidHom` from the multiplicative units of `K(X)` to `Multiplicative (Div(X))`
433	(equivalently: an additive map `(K(X)^×, ·) → (Div(X), +)`).
434	
435	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_hom` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131).
436	
437	iter-177 body: closes coordinate-wise from the DVR identities
438	`v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f) + v_Y(g)`, `v_Y(1) = 0`. The per-`Y` identities live in
439	`Scheme.RationalMap.order` via `Ring.ordFrac` (a `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero
440	hom) and `WithZero.log_mul` / `WithZero.log_one`. -/
441	noncomputable def principal_hom [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
442	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
443	    (X.functionField)ˣ →* Multiplicative X.WeilDivisor where
444	  toFun u :=
445	    Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal (↑u : X.functionField) (Units.ne_zero u))
446	  map_one' := by
447	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑1 _) = 1.
448	    -- Use `← ofAdd_zero` to rewrite RHS as `Multiplicative.ofAdd 0`,
449	    -- then `congr 1` reduces to `principal ↑1 _ = 0` (Finsupp equality),
450	    -- and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate identity, which
451	    -- closes by unfolding `order` to `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 1) = 0`
452	    -- via `map_one` and `WithZero.log_one`.
453	    rw [← ofAdd_zero]
454	    congr 1
455	    apply Finsupp.ext
456	    intro Y
457	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
458	        (((1 : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField)) = 0
459	    rw [Units.val_one]
460	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
461	    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
462	  map_mul' u v := by
463	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑(u*v) _) =
464	    --       Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑u _) * Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑v _).
465	    -- Use `← ofAdd_add` to rewrite RHS, then `congr 1` reduces to
466	    -- `principal ↑(u*v) _ = principal ↑u _ + principal ↑v _` (Finsupp
467	    -- equality), and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate
468	    -- identity `order Y (uv) = order Y u + order Y v`, which closes by
469	    -- `Ring.ordFrac (uv) = Ring.ordFrac u * Ring.ordFrac v` (`map_mul`)
470	    -- and `WithZero.log_mul` (the nonzero hypotheses come from
471	    -- `Units.ne_zero` and `map_ne_zero`).
472	    rw [← ofAdd_add]
473	    congr 1
474	    apply Finsupp.ext
475	    intro Y
476	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((↑(u * v) : X.functionField))
477	      = Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑u : X.functionField)
478	      + Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑v : X.functionField)
479	    rw [Units.val_mul]
480	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
481	    rw [map_mul]
482	    exact WithZero.log_mul
483	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero u))
484	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero v))
485	
486	/-- **Principal divisors on a complete nonsingular curve have degree zero**
487	(Hartshorne Corollary II.6.10, Stacks 0BE3).
488	
489	For every nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×` on a smooth proper curve `C`
490	over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
491	`deg(div(f)) = 0 ∈ ℤ`.
492	
493	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_deg_zero` (Hartshorne II.6 Cor. 6.10 p. 138).
494	
495	The proof (Hartshorne 6.10): if `f ∈ k̄` is constant then `div(f) = 0` and the
496	claim is trivial. Otherwise the inclusion `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` exhibits `K(C)` as a
497	finite extension of `k̄(f) ≅ k̄(t)`, so the corresponding morphism
498	`φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` is finite, `div(f) = φ^*([0] - [∞])`, and pullback along a
499	finite morphism multiplies degree by `deg(φ)`. Two auxiliary sub-lemmas
500	(finite morphism induced by a non-constant rational function; multiplicativity
501	of degree under finite pullback, Hartshorne II.6.9) are deferred to follow-up
502	iters of `RR.1`. -/
503	theorem principal_degree_zero {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
504	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
505	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
506	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
507	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
508	    (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0) :
509	    degree (principal f hf) = 0 := by
510	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10 (iter-178 partial). Case-split on whether every
511	  -- prime-divisor order of `f` vanishes. On a complete nonsingular curve,
512	  -- this case split exactly recovers Hartshorne's constant-vs-non-constant
513	  -- split: `(∀ Y, ord_Y f = 0)` ⟺ `f ∈ k̄ \ {0}` (a nowhere-vanishing
514	  -- rational function on a complete curve is constant; that direction is
515	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10's "if `f ∈ k̄` then `(f) = 0`").
516	  by_cases hconst : ∀ Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
517	  · -- Constant branch: every order vanishes ⟹ `principal f hf = 0` ⟹
518	    -- `degree (principal f hf) = degree 0 = 0`.
519	    have hprincipal_zero : principal f hf = 0 := by
520	      apply Finsupp.ext
521	      intro Y
522	      change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
523	      exact hconst Y
524	    rw [hprincipal_zero]
525	    unfold degree
526	    exact Finsupp.sum_zero_index
527	  · -- Non-constant branch (Hartshorne II.6.10 `f ∉ k̄`): the inclusion
528	    -- `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` defines a finite morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` via the
529	    -- function-field-determines-curve correspondence (Hartshorne I.6.12),
530	    -- and `principal f hf = φ^*([0] - [∞])`. Pullback along a finite
531	    -- morphism of curves multiplies degrees by `deg(φ)` (Hartshorne
532	    -- II.6.9), so `deg(principal f hf) = deg(φ) · deg([0] - [∞]) =

540	/-! ## §6. Positive part of a Weil divisor
541	
542	Iter-190 plan-phase Lane I Pin 2 corrective substrate. The carrier
543	`X.WeilDivisor = X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ` is a finitely supported integer-valued
544	function on prime divisors; the *positive part* `(D)_0` is obtained by
545	replacing each coefficient `n_Y` with `max(n_Y, 0)`. Equivalently this is
546	the lattice join `D ⊔ 0` in the pointwise lattice structure (the
547	`semilatticeSup` instance on `Finsupp` is noncomputable). The complementary
548	*negative part* `(D)_∞ := (-D)_0` then gives the canonical decomposition
549	`D = (D)_0 - (D)_∞` into a difference of effective divisors.
550	
551	Blueprint reference: `def:WeilDivisor_positivePart` /
552	`lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` of
553	`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §6 (iter-190 plan-phase additions).
554	-/
555	
556	/-- **Positive part of a Weil divisor**.
557	
558	`positivePart D` is the divisor obtained from `D` by replacing each
559	coefficient `n_Y` with `max(n_Y, 0)` (equivalently `D ⊔ 0` in the
560	pointwise lattice structure on `Finsupp`). For `D = div(f)` on a smooth
561	proper curve `C`, this recovers the divisor-of-zeros `(f)_0` — the
562	formal sum of the zeros of `f` with their multiplicities.
563	
564	The complementary `negativePart D := positivePart (-D)` then gives
565	`D = positivePart D - negativePart D` as a difference of effective
566	divisors.
567	
568	Implementation: `Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp)`, the
569	generic Mathlib packaging that maps a finsupp's coefficients through a
570	zero-preserving function. (The `D ⊔ 0` lattice form is equivalent but
571	requires the noncomputable `Finsupp.semilatticeSup` synthesis on
572	`PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ`; the explicit `mapRange` form is more transparent
573	to typeclass synthesis.)
574	
575	Blueprint reference: `def:WeilDivisor_positivePart` (project-bespoke;
576	chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §6, Hartshorne II.6.10 phrasing). -/
577	noncomputable def positivePart (D : X.WeilDivisor) : X.WeilDivisor :=
578	  Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp) D
579	
580	/-- The positive part of the zero divisor is zero. -/
581	@[simp]
582	lemma positivePart_zero : positivePart (0 : X.WeilDivisor) = 0 := by
583	  change Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp)
584	    (0 : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) = (0 : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
585	  exact Finsupp.mapRange_zero
586	
587	/-- **Degree of positive part as a sum of capped coefficients.** A purely
588	symbolic Mathlib manipulation: unfolding `positivePart`
589	(= `Finsupp.mapRange (max · 0)`) and `degree` (= `Finsupp.sum (fun _ n => n)`)
590	identifies `degree (positivePart D)` with the sum of `max (D Y) 0` over
591	the support of `D`.
592	
593	This is the iter-192 structural-reduction helper consumed by
594	`degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` below. The proof is one line
595	via `Finsupp.sum_mapRange_index` from
596	`Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.Finsupp.Basic`. -/
597	lemma degree_positivePart_eq_sum_max (D : X.WeilDivisor) :
598	    degree (positivePart D) = D.sum (fun _ n => max n 0) := by
599	  unfold positivePart degree
600	  exact Finsupp.sum_mapRange_index (h := fun _ b => b) (by intro _; rfl)
601	
602	/-- **Positive part of a `Finsupp.single`.** Pointwise extraction of the
603	mapRange definition of `positivePart`: for a one-point-supported Weil divisor
604	`Finsupp.single Y n`, the positive part is `Finsupp.single Y (max n 0)`.
605	A direct consequence of `Finsupp.mapRange_single` (with `max 0 0 = 0` as
606	the zero-preservation witness).
607	
608	iter-193 substrate helper paving the way for the Lane I body close. -/
609	@[simp]
610	lemma positivePart_single (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ) :
611	    positivePart (Finsupp.single Y n : X.WeilDivisor) =
612	      Finsupp.single Y (max n 0) := by
613	  change Finsupp.mapRange (fun n : ℤ => n ⊔ 0) (by simp)
614	      (Finsupp.single Y n) = _
615	  rw [Finsupp.mapRange_single]
616	
617	/-- **Degree of a `Finsupp.single` Weil divisor.** The degree of the
618	one-point-supported Weil divisor `Finsupp.single Y n` is `n`. A direct
619	consequence of `Finsupp.sum_single_index`.
620	
621	iter-193 substrate helper for `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`. -/
622	@[simp]
623	lemma degree_single (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ) :
624	    degree (Finsupp.single Y n : X.WeilDivisor) = n := by
625	  unfold degree
626	  change (Finsupp.single Y n : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n) = n
627	  exact Finsupp.sum_single_index rfl
628	
629	/-- **Sum-over-prime-divisors lower bound via a single contributing point.**
630	If `f` has order `1` at some prime divisor `Y₀`, then the degree of the positive
631	part of `principal f hf` is at least `1`.
632	
633	iter-193 substrate helper consumed by `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`:
634	it formalises Step 2 of the Hartshorne II.6.9 recipe (`hlp` produces
635	the local-parameter prime divisor `Y₀` with coefficient `1`). -/
636	lemma one_le_degree_positivePart_principal_of_order_one
637	    [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
638	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
639	    (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0) (Y₀ : X.PrimeDivisor)
640	    (h₀ : Scheme.RationalMap.order Y₀ f = 1) :
641	    1 ≤ degree (positivePart (principal f hf)) := by
642	  classical
643	  rw [degree_positivePart_eq_sum_max]
644	  -- The sum is over a finset; isolate the Y₀ contribution.
645	  have hY₀_supp :
646	      Y₀ ∈ (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).support := by
647	    rw [Finsupp.mem_support_iff]
648	    rw [principal_apply]
649	    rw [h₀]
650	    exact one_ne_zero
651	  -- For the sum to be ≥ 1, isolate the Y₀ term (= 1) and the rest (≥ 0).
652	  have h_split :
653	      (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).sum
654	          (fun _ n => max n 0) =
655	        max ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y₀) 0 +
656	          ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).support.erase Y₀).sum
657	            (fun Y => max
658	              ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y) 0) := by
659	    rw [show (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).sum
660	            (fun _ n => max n 0) =
661	          (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).support.sum
662	            (fun Y => max
663	              ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y) 0)
664	        from rfl]
665	    rw [Finset.sum_erase_eq_sub hY₀_supp]
666	    ring
667	  rw [h_split]
668	  -- The Y₀ term equals max 1 0 = 1.
669	  have hY₀_val :
670	      (show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y₀ = 1 := by
671	    rw [principal_apply]; exact h₀
672	  rw [hY₀_val]
673	  -- The remainder is a sum of non-negative integers, hence ≥ 0.
674	  have h_nonneg :
675	      0 ≤ ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf).support.erase Y₀).sum
676	        (fun Y => max
677	          ((show (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) from principal f hf) Y) 0) :=
678	    Finset.sum_nonneg (fun Y _ => le_max_right _ _)
679	  -- Conclude: 1 = max 1 0 ≤ max 1 0 + 0 ≤ LHS + remainder.
680	  have : (max (1 : ℤ) 0) = 1 := by norm_num
681	  rw [this]
682	  linarith
683	
684	/-- **Generic Finsupp identity**: the sum of clipped non-negative parts equals
685	the sum of the coefficients over the positive-coefficient sub-support.
686	
687	For a finsupp `D : α →₀ ℤ`, `D.sum (max · 0)` agrees with the unclipped sum
688	`∑ D` restricted to the finset `{a ∈ supp D | 0 < D a}`. Negative-coefficient
689	points contribute `0` to the max and drop out; positive-coefficient points
690	contribute `D a` to both sides; the (vacuously zero) `D a = 0` case is excluded
691	by the `supp` filter on both sides.
692	
693	iter-195 Lane I substrate helper for `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`:
694	it formalises the "the positive part of `D` is supported on the positive-coefficient
695	points" identity, which is Step 2.5 of the Hartshorne II.6.9 recipe (between the
696	`degree_positivePart_eq_sum_max` unfolding and the ramification-inertia chase). -/
697	lemma _root_.Finsupp.sum_max_zero_eq_sum_filter_pos {α : Type*}
698	    (D : α →₀ ℤ) :
699	    D.sum (fun _ n => max n 0) =
700	      ∑ a ∈ D.support.filter (fun a => 0 < D a), D a := by
701	  classical
702	  rw [Finsupp.sum, Finset.sum_filter]
703	  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
704	  intro a _
705	  by_cases hpos : 0 < D a
706	  · simp [hpos]
707	    omega
708	  · simp [hpos]
709	    omega

1110	/-! ## §7. Linear equivalence and the divisor class group -/
1111	
1112	/-- **Linear equivalence of Weil divisors.**
1113	
1114	Two Weil divisors `D, D' ∈ Div(X)` are linearly equivalent, written `D ~ D'`, if
1115	and only if there exists a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` with
1116	`D - D' = div(f) ∈ Div(X)`.
1117	
1118	`~` is an equivalence relation (reflexivity from `div(1) = 0`, symmetry from
1119	`div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, transitivity from `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, all via
1120	`thm:principal_hom`). The quotient `Cl(X) := Div(X) / im(div)` is the
1121	**divisor class group** of `X`.
1122	
1123	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, `thm:principal_deg_zero` shows the
1124	degree map descends to `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ`.
1125	
1126	Blueprint reference: `def:linear_equivalence` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131). -/
1127	def LinearEquivalence [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
1128	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (D D' : X.WeilDivisor) : Prop :=
1129	  ∃ (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0), D - D' = principal f hf
1130	
1131	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
1132	
1133	end AlgebraicGeometry

112	/-! ## §1. The line bundle of a closed point on a smooth proper curve
113	
114	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` and a closed
115	point `P ∈ C`, the local ring `𝒪_{C,P}` is a DVR with maximal ideal
116	generated by a uniformiser `f_P`. Hartshorne's construction `ℒ(D)`
117	(II §6 p. 144) applied to the Cartier divisor `[P]` (locally cut out by
118	`f_P` near `P`, by `1` elsewhere) produces the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)`:
119	the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)`
120	generated locally by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on `C \ {P}`. -/
121	
122	namespace Scheme

124	/-! ### Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C` carrier (iter-183 Lane A scaffold)
125	
126	The substantive iter-183 contribution: a concrete per-open `Set`-valued
127	carrier of the line bundle `𝒪_C(P)`, realised directly as Hartshorne's
128	subsheaf of the function-field constant sheaf `K_C` (Hartshorne II §6
129	p. 144; analogist `ocofp-sheaf-internalhom.md` Decision 3
130	`ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB`).
131	
132	A section of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U` is a rational function `f ∈ K(C)`
133	satisfying the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor
134	`Q ≠ P` with `Q.point ∈ U` (regularity on the complement of `P` inside
135	`U`) and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
136	The construction is independent of the choice of uniformiser at `P`:
137	any two uniformisers differ by a unit, so the order-`≥ −1` condition at
138	`P` is intrinsic.
139	
140	iter-184+ will upgrade `carrierSet` to a `Submodule kbar K(C)` via the

146	/-- **Carrier set** of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U : (Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`: the

153	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet
154	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]

160	    Set C.left.functionField := by

162	  haveI := Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instKrullDimLEStalk
163	    (X := C.left) Phat

168	/-- **Monotonicity of `carrierSet` in `U`**: when `V.unop ⊆ U.unop` (i.e.

171	open `V` involve fewer prime divisors, hence are easier to satisfy).

174	restriction map of the would-be `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.presheaf` functor:
175	in `(Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`, an arrow `U ⟶ V` corresponds to `V.unop ⊆ U.unop`,

238	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule

241	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
242	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
243	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
244	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
245	    Submodule kbar C.left.functionField where
246	  carrier := lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh U
247	  zero_mem' := by
248	    -- `order Y 0 = WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0`;
249	    -- both `0 ≤ 0` and `-1 ≤ 0` hold trivially.
250	    refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩
251	    · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
252	    · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
253	  add_mem' := by
254	    -- Case-split on `f + g = 0` (trivial via `WithZero.log_zero`), else
255	    -- apply `Ring.ordFrac_add` (DVR-shipped, Step 1 unlocks) and
256	    -- `WithZero.log` monotonicity on the nonzero part.
257	    rintro a b ⟨ha₁, ha₂⟩ ⟨hb₁, hb₂⟩
258	    have hMNZ : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (x : C.left.functionField),
259	        x ≠ 0 → Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point) x ≠ 0 := by
260	      intro Q x hx; simp [hx]
261	    have key : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ),
262	        n ≤ 0 → n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q a →
263	          n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q b →
264	          n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (a + b) := by
265	      intro Q n hn haₚ hbₚ
266	      by_cases hab : a + b = 0
267	      · simpa [hab, Scheme.RationalMap.order] using hn
268	      by_cases hae : a = 0
269	      · rw [hae, zero_add]; exact hbₚ
270	      by_cases hbe : b = 0
271	      · rw [hbe, add_zero]; exact haₚ
272	      set R := C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point
273	      have hoa : Ring.ordFrac R a ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q a hae
274	      have hob : Ring.ordFrac R b ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q b hbe
275	      have hoab : Ring.ordFrac R (a + b) ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q _ hab
276	      have hmin : Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b ≤ Ring.ordFrac R (a + b) :=
277	        Ring.ordFrac_add (R := R) a b hab
278	      have hlog : (Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b).log ≤
279	          Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (a + b) := by
280	        rcases min_cases (Ring.ordFrac R a) (Ring.ordFrac R b) with
281	            ⟨heq, _⟩ | ⟨heq, _⟩
282	        · rw [heq]; exact (WithZero.log_le_log hoa hoab).mpr (heq ▸ hmin)
283	        · rw [heq]; exact (WithZero.log_le_log hob hoab).mpr (heq ▸ hmin)
284	      have hminbd : n ≤ (Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b).log := by
285	        rcases min_cases (Ring.ordFrac R a) (Ring.ordFrac R b) with
286	            ⟨heq, _⟩ | ⟨heq, _⟩
287	        · rw [heq]; exact haₚ
288	        · rw [heq]; exact hbₚ
289	      linarith
290	    refine ⟨fun Q hQU hQP => key Q 0 le_rfl (ha₁ Q hQU hQP) (hb₁ Q hQU hQP),
291	      fun hPU => key ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ (-1) (by norm_num) (ha₂ hPU) (hb₂ hPU)⟩

293	    -- For `c : kbar`, the scalar action `c • f` on `K(C)` factors through

295	    -- a nonzero `β` in every stalk via the germ map; `Ring.ordFrac_ge_one_of_ne_zero`

299	    · -- `c = 0` ⇒ `0 • x = 0` ∈ carrierSet via `zero_mem'`.
300	      simp only [zero_smul]
301	      refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩ <;> simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
302	    rcases eq_or_ne x 0 with rfl | hx_ne
303	    · -- `x = 0` ⇒ `c • 0 = 0` ∈ carrierSet.
304	      simp only [smul_zero]
305	      refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩ <;> simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]

310	      have hinj := FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective kbar C.left.functionField
311	      simpa using hinj.ne_iff.mpr hc
312	    have hMNZ : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (y : C.left.functionField),
313	        y ≠ 0 → Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point) y ≠ 0 := by
314	      intro Q y hy; simp [hy]
315	    have key_alpha_ge : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor),
316	        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q
317	              (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) := by
318	      intro Q
319	      set R := C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point
320	      let β : R := (C.left.presheaf.germ (⊤ : C.left.Opens) Q.point trivial).hom
321	        ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C
322	            (Opposite.op (⊤ : C.left.Opens))).hom c)
323	      have hα_eq : (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) =
324	          algebraMap R C.left.functionField β := by
325	        change ((Scheme.germToFunctionField C.left (⊤ : C.left.Opens)).hom
326	            ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C
327	                (Opposite.op (⊤ : C.left.Opens))).hom c)) =
328	            (C.left.presheaf.stalkSpecializes
329	              ((genericPoint_spec C.left).specializes trivial)).hom β
330	        rw [← TopCat.Presheaf.germ_stalkSpecializes_apply
331	          (h := (genericPoint_spec C.left).specializes trivial)]
332	      have hβne : β ≠ 0 := by
333	        intro hzero; apply hαne; rw [hα_eq, hzero, map_zero]
334	      have hαord_ne : Ring.ordFrac R
335	          (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q _ hαne

337	          Ring.ordFrac R (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) := by
338	        rw [hα_eq]; exact Ring.ordFrac_ge_one_of_ne_zero hβne

340	      rw [show (0 : ℤ) = WithZero.log (1 : WithZero (Multiplicative ℤ)) by simp]
341	      exact (WithZero.log_le_log (by norm_num) hαord_ne).mpr hge
342	    have key : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ),
343	        n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q x →
344	        n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (c • x) := by
345	      intro Q n hxn
346	      rw [hsmul]

348	      have hαnez := hMNZ Q _ hαne
349	      have hxnez := hMNZ Q x hx_ne
350	      unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
351	      rw [map_mul, WithZero.log_mul hαnez hxnez]
352	      have := key_alpha_ge Q

355	    refine ⟨fun Q hQU hQP => key Q 0 (hx₁ Q hQU hQP),

419	/-- **Type-level Subfunctor presentation of the carrier** (iter-189 Subfunctor
420	restructure). The carrier of `𝒪_C(P)` viewed as a `CategoryTheory.Subfunctor` of
421	the Type-valued presheaf `TopCat.presheafToType C.left.toTopCat C.left.functionField`

423	an open `U` is a function `g : U.unop → K(C)` that is constant with value
424	`f ∈ carrierSubmoduleSheaf U`.

427	- At `U ≠ ⊥`: a section is a constant function valued in
428	  `carrierSubmoduleSheaf U = carrierSubmodule U` (so the order conditions hold).
429	- At `U = ⊥`: the unique empty function (witnessed by `f = 0 ∈ ⊥`).
430	
431	Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Subfunctor.isSheaf_iff`, applied against the ambient
432	sheaf `TopCat.Presheaf.toType_isSheaf` for `presheafToType`, reduces the sheaf
433	condition for the (type-valued shadow of the) carrier to a stalk-locality check:
434	every section of `presheafToType K(C)` whose sieve-of-section is covering already
435	lies in the Subfunctor. On the irreducible scheme `C.left`, this stalk-locality

448	    ∃ f : C.left.functionField,

455	    by_cases hV : V.unop = (⊥ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)

545	underlying `Type`-valued sheaf condition on its forget: each `carrierSubmodule U`

569	  Subfunctor-glue + stalk-locality close (irreducibility of `C.left.toTopCat`

885	  [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
886	  [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]

888	/-! ## §2. Global sections as the Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`
889	
890	Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7 identifies the global sections of
891	`ℒ(D_0)` with the rational functions `f ∈ K(X)^×` satisfying
892	`div(f) ≥ −D_0` (plus the zero section). Specialised to `D_0 = [P]` on a
893	curve, the condition `div(f) + [P] ≥ 0` rewrites coordinate-wise as
894	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`. -/
895	
896	/-- **Forward direction of `globalSections_iff` (Hartshorne II.7.7(b)).**
897	
898	Given `f ∈ K(C)^×` with `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P`
899	and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`, the rational function `f` lifts to a global section
900	`s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under the canonical inclusion
901	`𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` equals `f`. Hartshorne's construction inside the
902	proof of Proposition 7.7(b) (p. 157) defines this section locally on the
903	affine cover witnessing the Cartier-divisor structure of `[P]`: on a
904	neighbourhood `U_i` of `P` the section is `f · f_P` (in
905	`𝒪_C(P)(U_i) = f_P⁻¹ · 𝒪_C(U_i)`, this is `(f · f_P) · f_P⁻¹ = f`);
906	on the complement `C ∖ {P}` the section is `f` directly (the order
907	conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` guarantee `f ∈ 𝒪_C(U) = 𝒪_C(P)(U)`
908	on any affine open `U ⊆ C ∖ {P}`).
909	
910	**iter-187 closure**: now that `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` has its
911	substantive body (`⟨carrierPresheaf P hPcoh, carrierPresheaf_isSheaf
912	P hPcoh⟩`) and `toFunctionField` has its substantive body
913	(the `HModule_zero_linearEquiv → constantSheafAdj → carrierSubmodule`
914	chain), the forward direction is constructed via the *reverse* chain:
915	the order conditions `_hord` express exactly that `f ∈ carrierSubmodule
916	P hPcoh (op ⊤)`. From the witness `⟨f, hf_in⟩ : ↥(carrierSubmodule ...)`,
917	we build a `kbar`-linear map `kbar → carrierSubmodule(⊤)` via
918	`LinearMap.toSpanSingleton` (sending `1 ↦ ⟨f, hf_in⟩`), lift through
919	`constantSheafAdj.homEquiv.symm` into a sheaf morphism, then through
920	`HModule_zero_linearEquiv.symm` into the `HModule` element `s`. The
921	round-trip `toFunctionField s = f` follows from the unit/counit
922	equation `adj.homEquiv_unit` evaluated at the input `(1 : kbar)`. -/
923	private lemma globalSections_iff_mp
924	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
925	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
926	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
927	    (f : C.left.functionField) (_hf : f ≠ 0)
928	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
929	    (_hord : (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
930	        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
931	      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f) :
932	    ∃ s : Scheme.HModule kbar
933	        (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh) 0,
934	      lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField
935	        (C := C) P hP hPcoh s = f := by
936	  set hT : Limits.IsTerminal (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :=
937	    Preorder.isTerminalTop _
938	  have htop_ne_bot :
939	      (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) ≠ ⊥ := by
940	    intro h
941	    have hn :
942	        (((⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) : Set C.left.toTopCat)).Nonempty :=
943	      ⟨(inferInstance : Nonempty C.left).some, Set.mem_univ _⟩
944	    rw [h, TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_bot] at hn
945	    exact hn.ne_empty rfl
946	  have hf_in : f ∈ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf P hPcoh
947	      (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) := by
948	    refine ⟨⟨fun Q _ hQP => _hord.1 Q hQP, fun _ => _hord.2⟩, Or.inl htop_ne_bot⟩
949	  set F := lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh
950	  let φ : kbar →ₗ[kbar] ↑(lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf P hPcoh
951	      (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) :=
952	    LinearMap.toSpanSingleton kbar _ ⟨f, hf_in⟩
953	  let φ_cat : ModuleCat.of kbar kbar ⟶ F.val.obj
954	      (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) :=
955	    ModuleCat.ofHom φ
956	  set adj := (CategoryTheory.constantSheafAdj
957	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) hT)
958	  let sheafHom := (adj.homEquiv (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F).symm φ_cat
959	  let s : Scheme.HModule kbar F 0 :=
960	    (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F).symm sheafHom
961	  refine ⟨s, ?_⟩
962	  change ((((CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map
963	    ((Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F) s)).app
964	    (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).hom
965	      ((adj.unit.app (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).hom (1 : kbar))).1 = f
966	  have h1 : (Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F) s = sheafHom := by simp [s]
967	  rw [h1]
968	  have h3 := adj.homEquiv_unit (X := ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) (Y := F) (f := sheafHom)
969	  have h_simp : (adj.homEquiv (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F) sheafHom = φ_cat :=
970	    (adj.homEquiv _ _).apply_symm_apply _
971	  rw [h_simp] at h3
972	  -- `sheafSections.obj.map sheafHom = sheafToPresheaf.map sheafHom .app (op ⊤)`
973	  -- holds by `rfl` since `sheafSections := sheafToPresheaf.flip`.
974	  have hrew : ((CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf _ _).map sheafHom).app
975	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) =
976	      ((CategoryTheory.sheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
977	        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj
978	          (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).map sheafHom :=
979	    rfl
980	  rw [hrew]
981	  -- Use `h3` and `ModuleCat.comp_apply` to identify the goal's chain with `φ_cat.hom 1`.
982	  have h_eq : (((CategoryTheory.sheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
983	        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj
984	          (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))).map sheafHom).hom
985	        ((adj.unit.app (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).hom (1 : kbar)) = φ_cat.hom 1 := by
986	    rw [h3]; exact (ModuleCat.comp_apply _ _ _).symm
987	  -- Reduce to `(φ_cat.hom 1).1 = f` via the equation `h_eq`.
988	  -- (Direct `rw [h_eq]` fails to match due to subtle `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` vs `.hom`
989	  -- elaboration; we use `congr_arg Subtype.val h_eq` to lift the equation through
990	  -- the `↑` coercion explicitly.)
991	  refine (congr_arg Subtype.val h_eq).trans ?_
992	  -- Now the goal is `(φ_cat.hom 1).1 = f`. `φ_cat.hom = φ`, and `φ 1 = ⟨f, hf_in⟩`.
993	  change (φ 1).1 = f
994	  simp [φ, LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]
995	
996	/-- **Backward direction of `globalSections_iff` (Hartshorne II.7.7(a)).**
997	
998	Given a global section `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under
999	`𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` equals `f`, the order conditions on `f` follow by
1000	reading off the stalk-by-stalk DVR identification. Concretely:

10	/-!
11	# Vanishing of `H¹` for skyscraper sheaves on a curve (RR.2.H¹)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.2.H¹** project-side build of the closed-point
14	skyscraper `H¹`-vanishing identity
15	
16	  `dim_{k̄} H¹(C, skyscraperSheaf P (ModuleCat.of k̄ k̄)) = 0`
17	
18	on a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`. The file is
19	the iter-191 Lane H **file-skeleton**: each of the eight pinned
20	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching
21	the blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/RiemannRoch_H1Vanishing.tex`),
22	with `sorry` bodies; the iter-192+ closure follows the classical
23	Hartshorne III.2.5 flasque-vanishing argument.
24	
25	The chapter strategy (Hartshorne III §2): an injective resolution of a
26	flasque sheaf yields a flasque quotient at each stage; the
27	global-sections functor is exact on a flasque-to-flasque-to-flasque
28	short exact sequence; hence the right-derived global-sections functor
29	vanishes on a flasque input in positive degree. We specialise the
30	abstract flasque-vanishing to the closed-point skyscraper sheaf, which
31	is flasque because it is the pushforward, along the closed embedding of
32	a one-point subspace, of the constant sheaf on an irreducible base.
33	
34	## Eight pinned declarations
35	
36	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IsFlasque` — predicate on
37	   `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`.
38	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IsFlasque.pushforward` — pushforward of a
39	   flasque sheaf is flasque (Hartshorne II.1, Ex. 1.16(d)).
40	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IsFlasque.constant_of_irreducible` —
41	   constant sheaf on an irreducible space is flasque (Hartshorne
42	   II.1, Ex. 1.16(a)).
43	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule_flasque_eq_zero` — flasque sheaves
44	   have zero `HModule` in positive degree (Hartshorne III.2.5).
45	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward_const` —
46	   skyscraper sheaf is the pushforward of a constant sheaf along the
47	   closed embedding of the closure of the support point.
48	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PrimeDivisor.closure_isIrreducible` — the
49	   closure of the support point of a `PrimeDivisor` is irreducible.
50	7. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque` —
51	   closed-point skyscraper sheaf is flasque.
52	8. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero` — the
53	   `RR.2.H¹` headline (`dim_{k̄} H¹(C, k(P)) = 0`), obtained by composing
54	   declarations 4 and 7.
55	
56	The eighth declaration also lives as a `private` typed-`sorry` helper at
57	`AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean`. The `private` modifier
58	mangles the internal name of the RRFormula copy, so the public
59	declaration in this file is the one resolved by the blueprint's
60	`\lean{...}` pin and by downstream consumers (`sync_leanok` keys on the
61	fully-qualified public name).
62	
63	## References
64	
65	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_H1Vanishing.tex`.
66	Source: Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.1, Exercise 1.16
67	(flasque sheaves), Exercise 1.17 (skyscraper sheaves);
68	III.2, Proposition 2.5 (flasque sheaves are acyclic).
69	-/

80	/-! ## §1. Flasque sheaves of `kbar`-modules -/
81	
82	/-- **Flasque sheaf of `kbar`-modules on a topological space**
83	(Hartshorne II.1, Exercise 1.16; III.2, paragraph preceding Lemma 2.4).
84	
85	A sheaf `F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`
86	is **flasque** when, for every inclusion of opens `V ≤ U` in `X`, the
87	restriction map
88	`F.val.obj (op U) → F.val.obj (op V)` is surjective as a map of
89	`kbar`-modules.
90	
91	The predicate is read off the underlying presheaf of `kbar`-modules and
92	applies uniformly to the project's `ModuleCat kbar`-flavoured cohomology
93	pipeline (it does not depend on any topological-space hypothesis on `X`,
94	so the curve specialisation is by instance synthesis on
95	`C.left.toTopCat`).
96	
97	Blueprint reference: `def:isFlasque_sheaf`. -/
98	def Scheme.IsFlasque
99	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
100	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) : Prop :=
101	  ∀ ⦃U V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X⦄ (h : V ≤ U),
102	    Function.Surjective ((F.val.map (homOfLE h).op).hom)
103	
104	/-- **Pushforward of a flasque sheaf is flasque** (Hartshorne II.1,
105	Exercise 1.16(d)).
106	
107	For a continuous map of topological spaces `f : X ⟶ Y` and a flasque
108	sheaf `F` of `kbar`-modules on `X`, the pushforward sheaf `f _* F` is
109	flasque on `Y`. The reason is purely formal: the restriction map of
110	`f _* F` along `V ≤ U` in `Y` is, by definition of pushforward, the
111	restriction map of `F` along `f ⁻¹ V ≤ f ⁻¹ U` in `X`, which is
112	surjective by hypothesis on `F`.
113	
114	**iter-191 Lane H prover dispatch** — closed via the unfolding
115	`pushforward.map (homOfLE h).op = F.val.map ((Opens.map f).map (homOfLE h)).op`
116	(by `rfl`), so flasqueness of `F` along `(Opens.map f).map (homOfLE h)` gives
117	the conclusion. Blueprint reference: `lem:isFlasque_pushforward`. -/
118	theorem Scheme.IsFlasque.pushforward
119	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X Y : TopCat.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y)
120	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)}
121	    (hF : Scheme.IsFlasque F) :
122	    Scheme.IsFlasque
123	      ((TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) f).obj F) := by
124	  intro U V h
125	  exact hF ((Opens.map f).map (homOfLE h)).le
126	
127	/-- **Constant sheaf on an irreducible topological space is flasque**
128	(Hartshorne II.1, Exercise 1.16(a)).
129	
130	For an irreducible topological space `X`, every nonempty open is
131	connected and dense; the constant presheaf `U ↦ A` already satisfies the
132	sheaf condition, and its restriction maps are the identity on `A` (for
133	the nonempty branches) and factor through `0` (for the empty branch).
134	
135	**iter-191 Lane H file-skeleton** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry; closure
136	is iter-192+. Blueprint reference:
137	`lem:isFlasque_constant_irreducible`. -/
138	theorem Scheme.IsFlasque.constant_of_irreducible
139	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
140	    [IrreducibleSpace X] (A : ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :
141	    Scheme.IsFlasque
142	      ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
143	        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj A) := by
144	  -- iter-196 Lane H prover attempt: case analysis on `V = ⊥`.
145	  -- Empty branch (closed below): `F.val.obj (op ⊥)` is terminal in `ModuleCat kbar`
146	  -- by `TopCat.Sheaf.isTerminalOfEqEmpty`; hence the underlying type is
147	  -- `Subsingleton`, so any element of the codomain equals the image of `0`.
148	  -- Non-empty branch (residual): on an irreducible space, every non-empty open is
149	  -- connected/dense, so the sheafification unit
150	  -- `A → ((constantSheaf J D).obj A).val.obj (op V)` is iso for non-empty `V`;
151	  -- composition with the inverse on `V` and the unit on `U` lifts any
152	  -- `y ∈ F(V)` to a preimage in `F(U)`. Formalising this requires the
153	  -- plus-construction characterisation of sheafification on irreducible spaces
154	  -- (Mathlib snapshot `b80f227` does not ship a direct lemma).
155	  intro U V h y
156	  by_cases hV : V = ⊥
157	  · -- empty branch: codomain is terminal, hence Subsingleton.
158	    subst hV
159	    have hterm : Limits.IsTerminal
160	        (((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
161	            (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj A).val.obj
162	          (Opposite.op (⊥ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X))) :=
163	      TopCat.Sheaf.isTerminalOfEqEmpty
164	        ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
165	          (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj A) rfl
166	    have hzero : Limits.IsZero
167	        (((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
168	            (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj A).val.obj
169	          (Opposite.op (⊥ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X))) :=
170	      hterm.isZero
171	    have hsub : Subsingleton
172	        ((((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
173	            (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj A).val.obj
174	          (Opposite.op (⊥ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X))) : Type u) :=
175	      ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero hzero
176	    exact ⟨0, Subsingleton.elim _ _⟩
177	  · -- non-empty branch: requires sheafification-unit-iso on irreducible space.
178	    sorry

180	/-- **Injective sheaves have vanishing higher cohomology** (axiom-clean
181	helper, Hartshorne III §1).
182	
183	For a topological space `X` and an injective object `I` of the
184	`kbar`-module sheaf category, the `kbar`-flavoured derived
185	global-sections cohomology `HModule kbar I i` is the zero `kbar`-module
186	for every `i ≥ 1`. This is immediate from Mathlib's
187	`HasInjectiveDimensionLT` framework: `Injective I` gives
188	`HasInjectiveDimensionLT I 1` (Mathlib
189	`instHasInjectiveDimensionLTOfNatNatOfInjective`), and
190	`HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton` yields
191	`Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext Y I i)` for `i ≥ 1` and any `Y`; specialising
192	`Y` to the constant sheaf at `kbar` and applying
193	`Module.finrank_zero_of_subsingleton` closes the goal.
194	
195	**iter-192 Lane H prover dispatch** — closed axiom-clean. Used as the
196	`Ext^i(_, I) = 0` input inside the Hartshorne III.2.5 long-exact-sequence
197	chase for `HModule_flasque_eq_zero` below. -/
198	theorem Scheme.HModule_injective_finrank_eq_zero
199	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
200	    [HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
201	    [HasExt (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))]
202	    {I : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)}
203	    [Injective I] (i : ℕ) (hi : 1 ≤ i) :
204	    Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar I i) = 0 := by
205	  have hsub : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule kbar I i) :=
206	    HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton I 1 i hi _
207	  exact Module.finrank_zero_of_subsingleton

209	/-- **The canonical injective-embedding short exact sequence**
210	`0 → F → Injective.under F → cokernel(Injective.ι F) → 0` of sheaves of
211	`kbar`-modules (axiom-clean helper). The embedding `Injective.ι F` is
212	mono by `Injective.ι_mono`, the cokernel projection is epi by
213	construction, and the middle row is exact at `Injective.under F` by
214	`ShortComplex.exact_cokernel`. -/
215	noncomputable def Scheme.injectiveSES
216	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
217	    [HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
218	    [HasExt (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))]
219	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) :
220	    CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
221	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) :=
222	  ShortComplex.mk (Injective.ι F) (Limits.cokernel.π (Injective.ι F))
223	    (Limits.cokernel.condition (Injective.ι F))
224	
225	/-- The injective-embedding short complex is short exact. -/
226	theorem Scheme.injectiveSES_shortExact
227	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
228	    [HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
229	    [HasExt (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))]
230	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) :
231	    (Scheme.injectiveSES F).ShortExact :=
232	  { exact := ShortComplex.exact_cokernel (Injective.ι F)
233	    mono_f := Injective.ι_mono F
234	    epi_g := Limits.coequalizer.π_epi }
235	
236	/-- **Generic LES vanishing lemma**: in an abelian category with enough Ext,
237	if `0 → S.X₁ → S.X₂ → S.X₃ → 0` is short exact with `S.X₂` injective and the
238	post-composition `Hom(X, S.X₂) → Hom(X, S.X₃)` is surjective, then
239	`Ext X S.X₁ 1` is the zero `Subsingleton`.
240	
241	This is the structural skeleton of the Hartshorne III.2.5 argument
242	specialised at degree 1: the long exact sequence
243	`Ext X S.X₂ 0 → Ext X S.X₃ 0 → Ext X S.X₁ 1 → Ext X S.X₂ 1`
244	collapses because the rightmost term vanishes (injectivity of `S.X₂`)
245	and the leftmost map is surjective by the `Hom`-level hypothesis. The
246	proof composes:
247	- `HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton` (injective ⇒ `Ext X S.X₂ 1 = 0`).
248	- `Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁` (any `x₁ ∈ Ext X S.X₁ 1` lifts
249	  to some `x₃ ∈ Ext X S.X₃ 0` via the connecting morphism).
250	- `Abelian.Ext.addEquiv₀` (identifies `Ext X S.X₃ 0` with `Hom(X, S.X₃)`).
251	- `comp_extClass_assoc` (the LES "complex" identity used to discharge
252	  `(Ext.mk₀ S.g).comp extClass = 0`).
253	
254	**iter-192 Lane H prover dispatch** — closed axiom-clean. Used to peel
255	off the `i = 1` case of `HModule_flasque_eq_zero` once the
256	`Hom(X, I) → Hom(X, G)`-surjectivity input (Hartshorne II Ex. 1.16(b))
257	is supplied for an injective resolution. -/
258	theorem ext_one_eq_zero_of_hom_surjective_of_injective
259	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u} C] [Abelian C] [HasExt C]
260	    (X : C) {S : ShortComplex C} (hS : S.ShortExact) [Injective S.X₂]
261	    (hsurj : Function.Surjective (fun (f : X ⟶ S.X₂) => f ≫ S.g))
262	    (x₁ : Abelian.Ext X S.X₁ 1) : x₁ = 0 := by
263	  have hinj_subs : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext X S.X₂ 1) :=
264	    HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton S.X₂ 1 1 le_rfl _
265	  have hker : x₁.comp (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (show (1 : ℕ) + 0 = 1 by omega) = 0 :=
266	    Subsingleton.elim _ _
267	  obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
268	    Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ X hS x₁ hker
269	      (n₀ := 0) (show 0 + 1 = 1 by omega)
270	  obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := hsurj (Abelian.Ext.addEquiv₀ x₃)
271	  simp only at hy
272	  have hx3eq : Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (Abelian.Ext.addEquiv₀ x₃) = x₃ := by
273	    rw [← Abelian.Ext.addEquiv₀_symm_apply, AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
274	  have hx3_factored :
275	      x₃ = (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ y).comp (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.g)
276	        (show (0 : ℕ) + 0 = 0 by omega) := by
277	    rw [Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀, hy, hx3eq]
278	  rw [← hx₃, hx3_factored]
279	  rw [Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ y) (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.g) hS.extClass
280	      (show (0 : ℕ) + 0 = 0 by omega) (show (0 : ℕ) + 1 = 1 by omega)
281	      (show (0 : ℕ) + 0 + 1 = 1 by omega)]
282	  have hSg_extClass_zero :
283	      (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.g).comp hS.extClass (show (0 : ℕ) + 1 = 1 by omega) = 0 := by
284	    have key := hS.comp_extClass_assoc (Y := S.X₁) (n := 0)
285	      (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (𝟙 S.X₁)) (n' := 1) (h := show (1 : ℕ) + 0 = 1 by omega)
286	    rw [Abelian.Ext.comp_mk₀_id] at key
287	    exact key
288	  rw [hSg_extClass_zero, Abelian.Ext.comp_zero]
289	
290	/-- **Higher-degree LES vanishing lemma** (axiom-clean structural helper,
291	iter-193 Lane H prover dispatch).
292	
293	Given a short exact sequence `0 → S.X₁ → S.X₂ → S.X₃ → 0` in an abelian
294	category with injective `S.X₂` and `n₀ ≥ 1`, if every element of
295	`Abelian.Ext X S.X₃ n₀` is zero, then so is every element of
296	`Abelian.Ext X S.X₁ (n₀ + 1)`. This is the higher-degree analogue of
297	`ext_one_eq_zero_of_hom_surjective_of_injective`: at degrees `n₀ ≥ 1` the
298	`Hom`-surjectivity hypothesis is replaced by the (stronger, recursable)
299	"`Ext^{n₀}(_, S.X₃) = 0`" hypothesis.
300	
301	**Proof structure**: by `HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton` applied to
302	`S.X₂` injective at degree `n₀ + 1 ≥ 2`, `Abelian.Ext X S.X₂ (n₀ + 1)` is
303	subsingleton, so the LES bracket `Ext X S.X₃ n₀ →ᵟ Ext X S.X₁ (n₀+1) →
304	Ext X S.X₂ (n₀+1)` makes the connecting morphism δ surjective.
305	Combined with the source `Ext X S.X₃ n₀ = 0`, the conclusion follows.
306	Used in the `i ≥ 2` case of `Scheme.HModule_flasque_eq_zero`. -/
307	theorem ext_succ_eq_zero_of_injective_of_lower_zero
308	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u} C] [Abelian C] [HasExt C]
309	    (X : C) {S : ShortComplex C} (hS : S.ShortExact) [Injective S.X₂]
310	    {n₀ : ℕ} (h_n₀ : 1 ≤ n₀)
311	    (hX₃ : ∀ y : Abelian.Ext X S.X₃ n₀, y = 0)
312	    (x₁ : Abelian.Ext X S.X₁ (n₀ + 1)) : x₁ = 0 := by
313	  have hinj_subs : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext X S.X₂ (n₀ + 1)) :=
314	    HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton S.X₂ 1 (n₀ + 1) (by omega) _
315	  have hker : x₁.comp (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f)
316	      (show (n₀ + 1) + 0 = (n₀ + 1) by omega) = 0 :=
317	    Subsingleton.elim _ _
318	  obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
319	    Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ X hS x₁ hker (rfl : n₀ + 1 = n₀ + 1)
320	  rw [hX₃ x₃] at hx₃
321	  rw [← hx₃, Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]

323	/-! ### iter-194 Lane H substrate: forget₂ infrastructure for the
324	Hartshorne II.1.16(b) bridge
325	
326	The two helpers below set up the `forget₂ (ModuleCat kbar) AddCommGrpCat`
327	bridge that lets the project's `Scheme.IsFlasque` data feed into
328	Mathlib's `TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque.epi_of_shortExact` lemma.
329	
330	The bridge is **structural**: `sheafCompose J (forget₂ ModuleCat AddCommGrpCat)`
331	is additive (hence preserves zero morphisms) and is a functor between
332	the corresponding sheaf categories. Its action on a sheaf
333	`F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat kbar)` produces an `AddCommGrpCat`-valued
334	sheaf whose section-level data is the underlying additive group of
335	the original — so `Function.Surjective` of section-level maps transfers
336	trivially across the bridge.
337	
338	The remaining iter-194+ work is to lift `S.ShortExact` (at the
339	`ModuleCat kbar` level) to a corresponding `S.ShortExact` after
340	`sheafCompose forget₂` — the structural skeleton is in place; what's
341	missing is either `(sheafCompose forget₂).PreservesFiniteLimits` +
342	`PreservesFiniteColimits` instances (cleanest) or a direct port of
343	Mathlib's Zorn proof (~150-200 LOC). -/
344	
345	/-- Additive instance for `sheafCompose J F` when `F` is additive.
346	Lets us discharge `PreservesZeroMorphisms` for the
347	`sheafCompose (forget₂ ModuleCat AddCommGrpCat)` functor. -/
348	instance sheafCompose_additive {C : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category C]
349	    (J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C)
350	    {A B : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category A] [CategoryTheory.Category B]
351	    [CategoryTheory.Preadditive A] [CategoryTheory.Preadditive B]
352	    (F : A ⥤ B) [F.Additive] [J.HasSheafCompose F] :
353	    (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose J F).Additive where
354	  map_add := by
355	    intro X Y f g
356	    apply (CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf J B).map_injective
357	    change CategoryTheory.Functor.whiskerRight (f + g).hom F =
358	      CategoryTheory.Functor.whiskerRight f.hom F +
359	      CategoryTheory.Functor.whiskerRight g.hom F
360	    ext c
361	    simp
362	    rfl
363	
364	/-- `PreservesZeroMorphisms` instance for `sheafCompose J F` when `F`
365	is additive (derived from the `Additive` instance above). -/
366	instance sheafCompose_preservesZero {C : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category C]
367	    (J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C)
368	    {A B : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category A] [CategoryTheory.Category B]
369	    [CategoryTheory.Preadditive A] [CategoryTheory.Preadditive B]
370	    (F : A ⥤ B) [F.Additive] [J.HasSheafCompose F] :
371	    (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose J F).PreservesZeroMorphisms :=
372	  CategoryTheory.Functor.preservesZeroMorphisms_of_additive _
373	
374	/-- **iter-195 Lane H prover dispatch — single helper for SAb.Exact closure.**
375	`PreservesFiniteLimits` instance for `sheafCompose J F` when `F` preserves
376	finite limits, `A` and `B` are abelian and have finite limits, and the
377	sheaf categories have finite limits.
378	
379	The proof uses the canonical factorisation
380	`sheafCompose J F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J B = sheafToPresheaf J A ⋙ (whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F`
381	(by `rfl`): the right side preserves finite limits (each factor does), and
382	`sheafToPresheaf J B` reflects them (it is fully faithful, hence reflects isos),
383	so `sheafCompose J F` does too.
384	
385	This is the single helper that closes the `SAb.Exact` gap in
386	`Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective`: by
387	`Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono`, a
388	finite-limit-preserving additive functor between abelian categories takes
389	short exact sequences to exact short complexes. -/
390	instance sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits
391	    {C : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category C]
392	    (J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C)
393	    {A B : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category A] [CategoryTheory.Category B]
394	    [CategoryTheory.Limits.HasFiniteLimits A]
395	    [CategoryTheory.Limits.HasFiniteLimits B]
396	    (F : A ⥤ B) [CategoryTheory.Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits F]
397	    [J.HasSheafCompose F] :
398	    CategoryTheory.Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits
399	      (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose J F) := by
400	  have heq :
401	    (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose J F ⋙ CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf J B) =
402	      (CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf J A ⋙
403	        (CategoryTheory.Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F) := rfl
404	  haveI hcomp : CategoryTheory.Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits
405	      (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose J F ⋙ CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf J B) := by
406	    rw [heq]
407	    refine ⟨fun K _ _ => ?_⟩
408	    infer_instance
409	  exact CategoryTheory.Limits.preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves _
410	    (CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf J B)
411	
412	/-- **`Scheme.IsFlasque` transfers to `TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque` under
413	`sheafCompose forget₂`** (axiom-clean structural helper).
414	
415	The forget₂ functor `ModuleCat kbar → AddCommGrpCat` is faithful and
416	preserves the underlying function of every morphism. So
417	`Function.Surjective` of the underlying section-level restriction map
418	transfers across to `Epi` in `AddCommGrpCat` (via
419	`AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective`), and `Scheme.IsFlasque F` (which
420	says all restrictions are sectionwise surjective) directly produces
421	the `TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque` (which says all restrictions are epi). -/
422	theorem Scheme.IsFlasque.toAddCommGrpCat
423	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
424	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)}
425	    (hF : Scheme.IsFlasque F) :
426	    TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque
427	      ((CategoryTheory.sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
428	        (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj F) := by
429	  refine ⟨?_⟩
430	  intro U V i
431	  rw [AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective]
432	  intro y
433	  -- `i : U ⟶ V` in `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ` corresponds to `V.unop ≤ U.unop` in `Opens X`.
434	  have hle : V.unop ≤ U.unop := leOfHom i.unop
435	  obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := hF hle y
436	  exact ⟨x, hx⟩

438	/-- **Hartshorne II.1, Exercise 1.16(b)** (sections form): the
439	sheaf-morphism `S.g` is sectionwise-surjective on a short exact sequence
440	whose leftmost sheaf is flasque.
441	
442	For a sheaf-level short exact sequence
443	`0 → S.X₁ → S.X₂ → S.X₃ → 0` in `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
444	(ModuleCat kbar)` with `S.X₁` flasque, the section-level map
445	`(S.g.val.app (op U)).hom : S.X₂.val.obj (op U) → S.X₃.val.obj (op U)`
446	is surjective for every open `U`.
447	
448	**Proof sketch** (Hartshorne II.1 Ex 1.16(b), Zorn's lemma):
449	Let `t ∈ S.X₃.val.obj (op U)`. Consider pairs `(V, s)` where `V ⊆ U` is
450	open and `s ∈ S.X₂.val.obj (op V)` restricts to `t|_V`. Order by
451	extension. Any chain has an upper bound (sheaf gluing condition on
452	`S.X₂`). By Zorn's lemma, a maximal element `(V₀, s₀)` exists. Suppose
453	`V₀ ≠ U`. Pick `x ∈ U \ V₀`. Stalkwise, `S.X₂_x → S.X₃_x` is surjective
454	(SES in the abelian sheaf category preserves stalks). So in some
455	neighborhood `W` of `x` (after shrinking), there is
456	`s'_W ∈ S.X₂.val.obj (op W)` with image `t|_W`. On `V₀ ∩ W`,
457	`s₀ - s'_W` lies in `S.X₁.val.obj (op (V₀ ∩ W))` (since both map to
458	`t|_{V₀∩W}`). Use flasqueness of `S.X₁` to extend the difference to
459	`α ∈ S.X₁.val.obj (op W)`. Set `s'' := s'_W + α`. Then `s''` agrees with
460	`s₀` on `V₀ ∩ W`. By sheaf gluing on `S.X₂`, get a unique
461	`s''' ∈ S.X₂.val.obj (op (V₀ ∪ W))` extending both. This contradicts
462	maximality. Hence `V₀ = U`.
463	
464	**iter-194 Lane H prover dispatch** — body closed via the **forget₂**
465	trick: convert the project-side `Scheme.IsFlasque` data to Mathlib's
466	`TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque` predicate (on `AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves)
467	via the `sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (forget₂
468	(ModuleCat kbar) AddCommGrpCat)` functor, then invoke Mathlib's
469	`TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque.epi_of_shortExact` which is exactly this lemma
470	at the `AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaf level. The underlying function of
471	the section-level morphism is the same before and after `forget₂`, so
472	`Function.Surjective` at the `ModuleCat kbar` level transfers from
473	`Epi` at the `AddCommGrpCat` level via `ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective`
474	respectively `AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective`. Blueprint reference:
475	out-of-scope subsection of `thm:H1_vanishing_flasque` (the
476	Hartshorne II.1 Ex 1.16(b) input). -/
477	theorem Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective
478	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
479	    [HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
480	    {S : CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
481	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))}
482	    (hS : S.ShortExact)
483	    (hF : Scheme.IsFlasque S.X₁)
484	    (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
485	    Function.Surjective ((S.g.hom.app (Opposite.op U)).hom) := by
486	  -- Set up the forget₂ functor at the sheaf level.
487	  set Fforget := CategoryTheory.sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
488	    (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) with hFforget
489	  -- Build the AddCommGrpCat-valued ShortComplex via `S.map Fforget`.
490	  -- (Lean accepts this because `Fforget` has `PreservesZeroMorphisms` via the
491	  -- `sheafCompose_preservesZero` instance above.)
492	  set SAb : CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
493	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
494	    S.map Fforget with hSAb
495	  -- The IsFlasque hypothesis transfers across `forget₂`.
496	  haveI : TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque SAb.X₁ := hF.toAddCommGrpCat
497	  -- Mathlib's `IsFlasque.epi_of_shortExact` applies once we exhibit
498	  -- `SAb.ShortExact`. We decompose into its three components.
499	  -- (a) `Mono SAb.f` — provable axiom-clean using
500	  --     `Sheaf.Hom.mono_of_presheaf_mono` + `NatTrans.mono_of_mono_app`
501	  --     (since `forget₂` preserves underlying-function-level mono).
502	  -- (b) `Epi SAb.g` — provable axiom-clean using
503	  --     `Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi'` + the fact that
504	  --     `IsLocallySurjective` only depends on underlying types (so transfers
505	  --     across `forget₂`).
506	  -- (c) `SAb.Exact` — the remaining gap. Requires
507	  --     `(sheafCompose forget₂).PreservesHomology` (specifically
508	  --     preservation of kernels and cokernels in the sheaf category).
509	  haveI hMono_SAb_f : CategoryTheory.Mono SAb.f := by
510	    haveI : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f := hS.mono_f
511	    haveI hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono (Fforget.map S.f).hom := by
512	      change CategoryTheory.Mono
513	        (CategoryTheory.Functor.whiskerRight S.f.hom
514	          (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}))
515	      apply CategoryTheory.NatTrans.mono_of_mono_app
516	    change CategoryTheory.Mono (Fforget.map S.f)
517	    exact CategoryTheory.Sheaf.Hom.mono_of_presheaf_mono _ _ _
518	  haveI hEpi_SAb_g : CategoryTheory.Epi SAb.g := by
519	    haveI : CategoryTheory.Epi S.g := hS.epi_g
520	    change CategoryTheory.Epi (Fforget.map S.g)
521	    rw [← CategoryTheory.Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi']
522	    haveI hf : CategoryTheory.Sheaf.IsLocallySurjective S.g := by
523	      rw [CategoryTheory.Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi']
524	      infer_instance
525	    refine ⟨?_⟩
526	    intro V s
527	    exact @CategoryTheory.Presheaf.imageSieve_mem _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ S.g.hom hf _ s
528	  have hSAb_exact : SAb.ShortExact :=
529	    -- **iter-195 Lane H prover dispatch — closed via single helper.**
530	    -- Use `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono`
531	    -- in the forward direction: since `Fforget` preserves finite limits
532	    -- (helper `sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits` above) and is additive
533	    -- (helper `sheafCompose_additive`), it sends any `ShortExact` complex
534	    -- to an exact short complex.
535	    { exact := ((CategoryTheory.Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono
536	        Fforget).mp inferInstance S hS).1
537	      mono_f := hMono_SAb_f
538	      epi_g := hEpi_SAb_g }
539	  -- Apply Mathlib's lemma to get `Epi (SAb.g.hom.app (op U))`.
540	  haveI : CategoryTheory.Epi (SAb.g.hom.app (Opposite.op U)) :=
541	    TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque.epi_of_shortExact hSAb_exact
542	  -- Convert Epi to surjectivity at the AddCommGrpCat level.
543	  have hSurj_Ab : Function.Surjective ((SAb.g.hom.app (Opposite.op U))) :=
544	    (AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective _).mp inferInstance
545	  -- The section-level morphism `SAb.g.hom.app (op U)` is precisely the
546	  -- image of `S.g.hom.app (op U)` under `forget₂`. The underlying function
547	  -- is the same — `forget₂ ModuleCat AddCommGrpCat` preserves underlying types.
548	  -- So surjectivity transfers.
549	  intro y
550	  obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := hSurj_Ab y
551	  exact ⟨x, hx⟩
552	
553	/-- **Hartshorne II.1, Exercise 1.16(c)** (project-side cokernel
554	inheritance, axiom-clean):
555	
556	The cokernel of a flasque-by-flasque short exact sequence is flasque.
557	
558	For a sheaf-level short exact sequence
559	`0 → S.X₁ → S.X₂ → S.X₃ → 0` in `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
560	(ModuleCat kbar)` with both `S.X₁` and `S.X₂` flasque, `S.X₃` is also
561	flasque. The hypothesis `h_b` packages the Hartshorne II.1 Ex 1.16(b)
562	sections-surjectivity input as a parameter (rather than calling
563	`Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective` directly), keeping this
564	lemma's axiom-set clean — `sorryAx` traces only through the consumer
565	site, not through this declaration.
566	
567	**Proof**: for `V ≤ U`, given `t ∈ S.X₃.val.obj (op V)`, lift via `h_b` at
568	`V` to `t̃ ∈ S.X₂.val.obj (op V)`, extend via flasqueness of `S.X₂` from
569	`V` to `U` getting `T̃ ∈ S.X₂.val.obj (op U)`, then set
570	`T := S.g.val.app (op U) T̃ ∈ S.X₃.val.obj (op U)`. The restriction of
571	`T` to `V` equals `t` by naturality of `S.g.val` and the lift property of
572	`t̃`.
573	
574	**iter-193 Lane H prover dispatch** — closed axiom-clean. Used in the
575	`i ≥ 2` case of `HModule_flasque_eq_zero` to inherit flasqueness on the
576	quotient `G = cokernel(Injective.ι F)`. Blueprint reference: substrate
577	input in proof of `thm:H1_vanishing_flasque`. -/
578	theorem Scheme.IsFlasque.cokernel_of_shortExact_flasque_flasque
579	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
580	    [HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
581	    {S : CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
582	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))}
583	    (hI : Scheme.IsFlasque S.X₂)
584	    (h_b : ∀ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X),
585	      Function.Surjective ((S.g.hom.app (Opposite.op U)).hom)) :
586	    Scheme.IsFlasque S.X₃ := by
587	  intro U V hVU t
588	  obtain ⟨tLift, htLift⟩ := h_b V t
589	  obtain ⟨TExt, hTExt⟩ := hI hVU tLift
590	  refine ⟨(S.g.hom.app (Opposite.op U)).hom TExt, ?_⟩
591	  have nat := S.g.hom.naturality_apply (homOfLE hVU).op TExt
592	  -- nat : (S.g.hom.app (op V)).hom ((S.X₂.val.map _).hom TExt) =
593	  --       (S.X₃.val.map _).hom ((S.g.hom.app (op U)).hom TExt)
594	  rw [hTExt, htLift] at nat
595	  exact nat.symm
596	
597	/-- **Hartshorne III, Lemma 2.4** (Tier-3 typed sorry): every injective
598	sheaf of `kbar`-modules on `X` is flasque.
599	
600	For an injective sheaf `I` of `kbar`-modules on `X`, `I` is flasque as a
601	sheaf. The classical proof uses the extension-by-zero `j_!` functor: for
602	`V ⊆ U` open, the open immersion `j_V : V ↪ X` gives `j_{V!}(O_V)
603	↪ j_{U!}(O_U)`, and `Hom(j_{U!}(O_U), I) → Hom(j_{V!}(O_V), I)` is
604	surjective by injectivity of `I`. Translating via the
605	`j_{(-)!}` ⊣ `j_{(-)}*` adjunction (or the equivalent presheaf form for
606	the constant ring sheaf `kbar`), this gives surjectivity of `I(U) → I(V)`.
607
